root.name: PCMB
root.description: Career paths for In-School — PCMB in India

children.name: Medicine and Clinical Sciences
children.description: Patient-care careers requiring Biology at Class 12 and NEET-UG as the gateway exam.

children.name: MBBS (Allopathy)
children.description: India's primary medical degree — 5.5 years including one year of compulsory rural internship.

children.name: Clinical Specialist via MD or MS
children.description: Pursue NEET-PG after internship to specialise in medicine, surgery, paediatrics, gynaecology, or any of 20+ branches.

children.name: General Surgeon (MS Surgery)
children.description: Perform abdominal, laparoscopic, and emergency surgeries at hospitals. One of the most respected and in-demand clinical specialities in India.
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → MBBS → internship → NEET-PG → MS Surgery 3 yrs → senior residency → consultant surgeon

children.name: Physician or Specialist (MD Internal Medicine)
children.description: Diagnose and manage complex medical conditions — diabetes, cardiac, respiratory — as a physician or internal medicine specialist at hospitals or own clinic.
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → MBBS → NEET-PG → MD Internal Medicine → physician or DM super-speciality → consultant

what_it_is: Postgraduate specialisation that defines your clinical identity for life.
who_its_not_for: Those who cannot handle another 3 years of high-pressure residency.
work_lifestyle: Residency is intense; post-qualification is rewarding and independent.
entry_route: NEET-PG after MBBS internship; All India rank determines branch and college.
timeline: 3 years MD or MS; then fellowship or direct practice.
salary_range: ₹20L–₹80L / yr depending on speciality and city.
growth_and_progression: Consultant → senior consultant → department head or own hospital.
demand_and_outlook: High; surgical and procedural specialities always oversubscribed with patients.
honest_caveat: Government PG seats very few; bond service in rural areas often mandatory.

children.name: Super-Speciality via DM or MCh
children.description: After MD or MS, pursue a 3-year super-speciality (DM Cardiology, MCh Neurosurgery etc.) for the highest clinical prestige and income in India.
children.roadmap: MBBS → MD or MS → NEET-SS → DM or MCh 3 yrs → super-specialist → own practice or senior hospital position

what_it_is: Train to become a fully licensed doctor recognised by NMC.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to invest 10+ years before peak earning begins.
work_lifestyle: Gruelling during residency; varied and rewarding in independent practice.
entry_route: NEET-UG; government college requires 600+ for general category students.
timeline: MBBS 5.5 yrs then NEET-PG then MD or MS another 3 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹20L government; ₹20L–₹80L private specialist practice.
growth_and_progression: Registrar → consultant → senior consultant → department head.
demand_and_outlook: Perennially high; every rural and urban area needs more doctors.
honest_caveat: Medical colleges cap at 100–150 seats; barely 90,000 MBBS seats nationally.

children.name: BDS (Bachelor of Dental Surgery)
children.description: Five-year dental degree with lower NEET cutoff than MBBS and strong own-practice income potential after 5–7 years.

children.name: General Dentist and Clinic Owner
children.description: Register with DCI after BDS internship and build an independent or chain dental clinic — the most profitable long-term dental career in India.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹50L / yr (junior associate to established clinic owner)
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → BDS 5 yrs → internship → DCI registration → associate dentist → own clinic in 5–8 years

children.name: Dental Specialist via MDS
children.description: Specialise in orthodontics, oral and maxillofacial surgery, periodontics, or endodontics via NEET-MDS for premium metro clinical practice.
children.roadmap: BDS → internship → NEET-MDS → MDS 3 yrs → specialist practice or dental college teaching post

what_it_is: Train as a licensed dentist with surgical and clinical skills.
who_its_not_for: Those seeking large-hospital general medicine or research careers.
work_lifestyle: Clinic-based; hands-on; patient flow driven; own practice is end goal.
entry_route: NEET-UG; lower cutoff than MBBS; government seats in every state.
timeline: 5 yrs BDS including internship; MDS specialisation adds 3 more.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L employed; ₹15L–₹50L own established practice.
growth_and_progression: General dentist → MDS specialist → clinic chain owner or academic.
demand_and_outlook: Steady and growing; dental insurance penetration rising across India.
honest_caveat: Own practice requires significant capital and a good location to succeed.

children.name: BAMS or BHMS (Ayurveda or Homeopathy)
children.description: 5.5-year alternative medicine degrees accessible via NEET-UG with a lower cutoff — recognised by government and popular in tier-2 and rural India.

children.name: AYUSH Practitioner and Wellness Entrepreneur
children.description: Build an Ayurveda or Homeopathy clinical practice or wellness venture — a growing sector with rising urban demand and government AYUSH mission backing.

children.name: Ayurveda Doctor and Panchkarma Specialist
children.description: Run an Ayurveda clinic offering classical treatments, Panchkarma detox therapies, and lifestyle medicine — high demand in metro wellness centres and abroad.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹25L / yr (metro wellness clinic at higher end)
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → BAMS 5.5 yrs → internship → CCIM registration → associate at Ayurveda hospital → own clinic or wellness centre

children.name: AYUSH Government Hospital Physician
children.description: Join a government AYUSH hospital or dispensary as a medical officer — a stable salaried role with pension and no capital risk of private practice.
children.roadmap: BAMS or BHMS → internship → state AYUSH recruitment exam → medical officer at government dispensary → senior MO

what_it_is: Traditional medicine practice with government recognition and own-clinic scope.
who_its_not_for: Those who want evidence-based allopathy or hospital-system careers.
work_lifestyle: Own clinic or wellness centre; patient consultations; herbal product advisory.
entry_route: BAMS or BHMS via NEET-UG; lower cutoff than MBBS; AYUSH council seats.
timeline: 5.5 yrs degree; own practice viable within 2–3 yrs of registration.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹30L / yr own practice; ₹4L–₹12L employed at AYUSH hospital.
growth_and_progression: General practitioner → speciality clinic → wellness chain or herbal brand.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; government AYUSH hospitals and wellness tourism sector growing.
honest_caveat: Prescription rights narrower than MBBS; urban acceptance still building.

what_it_is: Traditional medicine degrees with government recognition and AYUSH scope.
who_its_not_for: Those who want evidence-based allopathy or research-heavy careers.
work_lifestyle: Clinic and pharmacy mix; own practice viable; less hospital rotation.
entry_route: NEET-UG; lower cutoff; state and central AYUSH council seats available.
timeline: 5.5 yrs including internship; PG via AYUSH PG entrance exam.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L employed; ₹10L–₹30L established own practice.
growth_and_progression: Own clinic, wellness centre, or AYUSH hospital employment track.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; government AYUSH hospitals and wellness tourism driving demand.
honest_caveat: Scope narrower than MBBS; not recognised for allopathic prescription rights.

what_it_is: Clinical degrees that train students to diagnose and treat human illness.
who_its_not_for: Those averse to blood, emotional burden, or decade-long study commitments.
work_lifestyle: Hospital rotations; long shifts; high responsibility and emotional intensity.
entry_route: NEET-UG; 720-mark single national exam; attempt limit removed post-2023.
timeline: MBBS 5.5 yrs; specialisation MD or MS adds 3 more years after that.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹80L depending on specialisation and public vs private setting.
growth_and_progression: Specialist, super-specialist, hospital director, or medical entrepreneur.
demand_and_outlook: Always high; India has roughly 1 doctor per 1,500 people still.
honest_caveat: Private MBBS seat costs ₹50L–₹1.5Cr; government seat is cutthroat.

children.name: Life Sciences and Biotechnology
children.description: Biology-driven degree paths leading to pharma, biotech, research, and health-tech careers.

children.name: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotechnology
children.description: Three or four-year degree combining biology, chemistry, and engineering principles for pharma and biotech industry careers.

children.name: Pharma Research and Drug Development
children.description: Work on drug discovery and formulation at India's major pharma companies — the R&D engine of a ₹4.5 lakh crore industry.

children.name: Drug Discovery Scientist
children.description: Work on identifying new drug candidates, running biological assays, and optimising lead molecules at pharma or biotech companies like Dr Reddy's Labs or Biocon.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech → M.Sc Pharma or Biochemistry → research associate at pharma company → scientist → senior scientist

children.name: Formulation Development Scientist
children.description: Develop oral solid dosage forms, injectables, and biologics formulations for regulated markets — the backbone of India's generic drug export industry.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm or B.Tech Biotech → M.Pharm Pharmaceutics → formulation scientist at Sun Pharma or Cipla → senior formulation scientist

what_it_is: Discovering, formulating, and testing new drugs for human use.
who_its_not_for: Those who need fast results or dislike repetitive experimental cycles.
work_lifestyle: Lab bench and documentation heavy; GMP-governed; publication-driven.
entry_route: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech then M.Sc Pharma or direct research associate hire.
timeline: Research associate on M.Sc; scientist in 3–4 yrs; senior scientist in 6–8.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹25L fresher to senior scientist range at Indian pharma firms.
growth_and_progression: Scientist → principal scientist → R&D project leader → R&D head.
demand_and_outlook: High; India files more global drug patents than any country except USA.
honest_caveat: PhD strongly preferred for senior scientist or director-level positions.

children.name: Clinical Research and Regulatory Affairs
children.description: Manage clinical trials, regulatory submissions, and pharmacovigilance for pharma companies and CROs operating in India and globally.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech → PG diploma Clinical Research → CRO internship → CRA → regulatory affairs manager

children.name: Bioinformatics and Computational Biology
children.description: Analyse genomic, proteomic, and drug-target data — the fastest-growing intersection of biology and data science in India and globally.

children.name: Genomics Data Scientist
children.description: Analyse whole-genome sequencing, RNA-seq, and single-cell data for research hospitals, genomics startups like MedGenome, or CSIR-IGIB in India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Bio → M.Sc Bioinformatics at NCBS or Hyderabad → genomics internship → data scientist at genomics firm or research lab

children.name: Computational Drug Discovery Scientist
children.description: Use molecular docking, MD simulation, and ML models to identify and optimise drug candidates — a cutting-edge role at pharma companies and AI-drug startups.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech → M.Sc or M.Tech Bioinformatics → computational chem internship → drug discovery scientist at biotech or AI-pharma firm

what_it_is: Using programming and statistics to decode biological data at scale.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike coding or abstract data analysis without wet lab work.
work_lifestyle: Mostly computational; collaborative with wet lab teams; publication-driven.
entry_route: B.Sc Bio or B.Tech Biotech plus Python self-learning; M.Sc Bioinformatics.
timeline: M.Sc Bioinformatics 2 yrs; scientist or analyst role after; PhD optional.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹35L; genomics and AI drug discovery pay at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Bioinformatics scientist → senior scientist → computational biology lead.
demand_and_outlook: Rising sharply; genomics, personalised medicine, and AI drug discovery boom.
honest_caveat: Must be genuinely comfortable coding; biology alone is not sufficient here.

what_it_is: Applying biological knowledge to make drugs, vaccines, and diagnostics.
who_its_not_for: Those who want immediate high-pay roles without postgraduate study.
work_lifestyle: Lab-heavy; GMP-aware; regulatory documentation is part of daily work.
entry_route: CUET for B.Sc; JEE Main for B.Tech Biotech at NITs and private colleges.
timeline: 3–4 yr degree; M.Sc or M.Tech or MBA Pharma adds 2 yrs more.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹12L after B.Sc or B.Tech; much higher post-M.Sc or PhD.
growth_and_progression: Research associate → scientist → principal scientist → R&D head.
demand_and_outlook: Good; India is the world's largest vaccine manufacturer with global pharma presence.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries low; patience and postgraduate study almost mandatory.

children.name: B.Sc in Zoology, Botany, or Microbiology
children.description: Pure biology degree leading to government service, teaching, or specialised research via postgraduate study.

children.name: Government Scientist or Forest Officer
children.description: Join India's national science agencies or forest departments as a technical officer — stable, respected, and mission-driven government careers.

children.name: ICAR Agricultural Scientist
children.description: Conduct crop improvement, soil science, or pest management research at Indian Council of Agricultural Research institutes spread across India.
skills: Agronomy or entomology or plant pathology
children.roadmap: B.Sc Botany or Agriculture → M.Sc → ICAR-ARS NET exam → ARS scientist posting → senior scientist

children.name: Indian Forest Service (IFS) Officer
children.description: Manage India's 7.12 lakh sq km of forest land — biodiversity conservation, wildlife protection, and tribal welfare — as a gazetted forest officer via UPSC.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹18L / yr (7th Pay Commission with forest allowances and housing)
children.roadmap: B.Sc or M.Sc Biology → UPSC Civil Services with Forestry optional → IFS allotment → IGNFA Dehradun training → DFO posting

what_it_is: Scientific or conservation work funded by Indian government bodies.
who_its_not_for: Those who want high salaries or fast promotion timelines.
work_lifestyle: Lab, field, and desk mix; structured government work pace.
entry_route: M.Sc then CSIR-NET or state science services exam; IFS via UPSC.
timeline: B.Sc 3 yrs + M.Sc 2 yrs + exam prep 1 yr; long but stable runway.
salary_range: ₹6L–₹15L / yr government scientist scale with non-cash perks.
growth_and_progression: Scientific officer → senior scientist → principal scientist → division head.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; ICAR, ICMR, and state forest services recruit regularly.
honest_caveat: Competitive exams required; promotions slow in government hierarchy.

children.name: Quality Control and Food Safety Analyst
children.description: Test food, pharma, and consumer goods for safety and regulatory compliance at FSSAI-registered labs and manufacturing companies across India.

children.name: Food Safety and Quality Analyst (FMCG)
children.description: Test raw materials, in-process samples, and finished products for microbiological and chemical safety at FMCG companies like Nestle, HUL, or Amul across India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Micro or Food Science → M.Sc Food Technology → QC trainee at FMCG company → analyst → senior QC analyst → manager

children.name: Pharmaceutical QC Analyst
children.description: Perform analytical testing of drug substances and drug products at pharma manufacturing plants, ensuring every batch meets pharmacopoeia and regulatory specifications.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Chemistry or Microbiology → M.Sc Analytical Chemistry or Pharma → QC analyst at pharma company → senior analyst

what_it_is: Ensuring every product reaching consumers meets safety standards.
who_its_not_for: Those who want patient care or fast-paced tech-style environments.
work_lifestyle: Lab bench; shift-based at factories; documentation and audit cycles.
entry_route: B.Sc Micro or Chemistry; M.Sc Food Tech or Microbiology preferred.
timeline: QC analyst on graduation; senior analyst in 3 yrs; manager in 5–7.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L / yr across food, pharma, and consumer goods sectors.
growth_and_progression: QC analyst → senior → QC manager → head of quality assurance.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; FSSAI regulations and export markets drive constant demand.
honest_caveat: Factory postings common; metro roles fewer and more competitive.

what_it_is: Foundational biology degree covering living organisms and their systems.
who_its_not_for: Those targeting engineering, medicine, or immediate industry roles.
work_lifestyle: Field surveys, lab work, and academic reading; slow-paced progression.
entry_route: CUET for central universities; state entrance exams for state colleges.
timeline: 3 yr B.Sc; M.Sc 2 yrs; PhD 5 more yrs for research career.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹8L after B.Sc; ₹8L–₹20L post-M.Sc in government or industry.
growth_and_progression: Government scientist, conservation officer, teacher, or quality analyst.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; government labs, forest departments, and food industry hire.
honest_caveat: Very limited industry roles after B.Sc alone; M.Sc is near-mandatory.

what_it_is: Biological sciences applied to medicine, agriculture, and industry.
who_its_not_for: Those who need high income immediately after a three-year degree.
work_lifestyle: Lab-intensive; project-based; publication or patent-driven progress.
entry_route: CUET for central universities; IISER IAT or KVPY for premier institutes.
timeline: 3-yr B.Sc or 4-yr B.Tech Biotech; M.Sc or PhD adds 2–5 yrs more.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L after B.Sc; ₹12L–₹40L post-M.Sc or PhD in industry.
growth_and_progression: Research scientist, regulatory affairs, clinical research, or biotech founding.
demand_and_outlook: Growing fast with India's pharma, vaccine, and genomics sectors booming.
honest_caveat: Pure B.Sc Biology alone limits options; M.Sc or specialisation is essential.

children.name: Engineering with Biology Intersection
children.description: Technical degrees that combine PCM skills with biological and health-science applications.

children.name: B.Tech Biomedical Engineering
children.description: Engineering degree designing medical devices, imaging systems, and diagnostics — where electronics meets human health.

children.name: Medical Device Design Engineer
children.description: Design, prototype, and validate devices such as ECG machines, ventilators, prosthetics, or point-of-care diagnostic kits.

children.name: Hardware Medical Device Engineer
children.description: Design and verify electronic circuits, sensors, and embedded firmware for diagnostic or therapeutic medical devices at MNC or Indian medtech companies.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Biomedical → intern at GE Healthcare or Philips India → hardware device engineer → senior engineer → R&D lead

children.name: Regulatory Affairs and Quality Engineer
children.description: Prepare technical files, 510(k) or CE mark submissions, and quality management systems for medical device market clearance in India and globally.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Biomedical → regulatory affairs training → QA or RA associate at device company → manager → director of regulatory affairs

what_it_is: Engineering physical products that save and improve patient lives.
who_its_not_for: Those who want software-only careers or fast startup culture.
work_lifestyle: Prototype lab and regulatory documentation; iterative physical design work.
entry_route: B.Tech Biomedical; intern at MNC device company; M.Tech helps for R&D.
timeline: Graduate engineer trainee on joining; senior engineer in 3–4 years.
salary_range: ₹6L–₹22L; GE Healthcare and Siemens Healthineers pay at top.
growth_and_progression: Design engineer → lead engineer → R&D manager → product director.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; India Medical Devices Policy targets ₹3.5L Cr market by 2030.
honest_caveat: Regulatory approval timelines are long; patience is essential in this field.

children.name: HealthTech and Digital Health Engineer
children.description: Build AI-powered diagnostics, telemedicine platforms, and hospital information systems at India's fast-growing health-tech startups and hospitals.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Biomedical or CSE → health-tech internship → engineer at Niramai or SigTuple or Practo → senior engineer or PM

what_it_is: Build the hardware and software behind hospitals' diagnostic equipment.
who_its_not_for: Those wanting pure patient care or pure software development roles.
work_lifestyle: R&D lab and regulatory paperwork; slower iteration cycles than software.
entry_route: JEE Main or state CET; limited seats at NITs and private colleges.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; M.Tech or MBA Pharma for senior roles typically.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L; GE Healthcare and Philips India pay at the higher end.
growth_and_progression: Device engineer → senior engineer → regulatory affairs → R&D head.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; India's Medical Devices Policy targets ₹3.5L Cr industry by 2030.
honest_caveat: Niche field; fewer campus recruiters than CS; regulatory learning steep.

children.name: B.Tech Genetic Engineering or Biotechnology (Specialised)
children.description: Four-year engineering degree focused on genetic modification, bioprocessing, and industrial biology — gateway to biotech and biopharma industry.

children.name: Bioprocess and Fermentation Engineer
children.description: Scale up biological production of vaccines, monoclonal antibodies, and recombinant proteins at Indian biopharma companies like Serum Institute or Biocon.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Biotech or Genetic Engineering → bioprocess internship at biopharma → associate engineer → bioprocess engineer → senior engineer

children.name: Synthetic Biology and Genome Engineering
children.description: Engineer microorganisms, gene circuits, and CRISPR-based tools for therapeutics, industrial biochemicals, or agricultural biotechnology applications.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹30L / yr (higher in US or European-funded startups)
children.roadmap: B.Tech Genetic Engineering → M.Sc or PhD in molecular biology → research associate at biotech startup or CSIR lab → scientist

what_it_is: Engineering living systems for medicine, agriculture, and industrial use.
who_its_not_for: Those who want immediate high pay or dislike detailed lab documentation.
work_lifestyle: Bioprocess lab; sterile technique; GMP-governed production environments.
entry_route: JEE Main or state CET; Amrita, VIT, and Manipal have strong programmes.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; M.Tech Bioprocess or MBA Pharma for industry leadership.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L; biopharma and vaccine companies pay highest in this sector.
growth_and_progression: Research associate → bioprocess engineer → production manager → plant head.
demand_and_outlook: High; Serum Institute, Bharat Biotech, and Biocon always hiring.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries lower than CS; postgraduate study speeds up progression.

children.name: B.Tech Food Technology or Agricultural Engineering
children.description: Engineering degree applying science to food processing, safety, and agricultural production — India's largest employment sector.

children.name: Food Processing and Quality Engineer
children.description: Manage production lines, quality systems, and regulatory compliance at FMCG companies like Nestle, ITC, or Britannia across India.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Food Tech → campus placement at FMCG company → production or QA engineer → plant manager in 8–10 yrs

children.name: Agri-Tech Entrepreneur or Agribusiness Manager
children.description: Build or manage technology-driven agricultural ventures — precision farming, cold chain logistics, or farmer-facing apps and input companies.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹20L employed; equity upside if founding a startup
children.roadmap: B.Tech Agri → MBA Agribusiness or directly join agri-tech startup → product or operations role → founding or leadership track

what_it_is: Engineering food products, agricultural machinery, and supply chains.
who_its_not_for: Those who want glamorous tech or finance careers in metro cities.
work_lifestyle: Factory floor, lab, and field mix; operations-heavy with travel.
entry_route: JEE Main or state CET; ICAR AIEEA for agricultural university programmes.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; M.Tech or MBA Agribusiness for management roles.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹18L; MNC FMCG companies and agri-tech startups pay best.
growth_and_progression: Production engineer → plant manager → operations head → agri-entrepreneur.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India processes only 10% of its food — massive scope for growth.
honest_caveat: Factory postings often in tier-3 cities; lifestyle differs from IT careers.

what_it_is: Engineering disciplines that solve health, food, and environment problems.
who_its_not_for: Those who want either pure biology or pure computer science only.
work_lifestyle: Lab and design studio mix; regulated industries with documentation demands.
entry_route: JEE Main for NITs; state CETs; NEET for some Biomedical programmes.
timeline: 4-year B.Tech; M.Tech or MBA adds 2 more years for senior roles.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹25L; MNC medical device and food tech companies pay best.
growth_and_progression: Senior engineer, regulatory specialist, R&D lead, or product manager.
demand_and_outlook: Rising; healthcare technology and agri-tech are government priority sectors.
honest_caveat: Smaller talent market than CS means fewer companies at campus placements.

children.name: Pharmacy and Allied Health Sciences
children.description: Shorter clinical and pharmaceutical degree paths accessible via NEET or direct state entrances.

children.name: B.Pharm (Bachelor of Pharmacy)
children.description: Four-year pharmacy degree covering drug formulation, pharmacology, and quality assurance — gateway to pharma industry and hospital pharmacy.

children.name: Pharma Industry Roles (Production or QA)
children.description: Work in drug manufacturing, quality assurance, or regulatory affairs at Indian pharma companies — the well-paying side of a pharmacy degree.

children.name: Production Pharmacist
children.description: Operate and oversee manufacturing of tablets, capsules, injectables, or API at pharma plants; ensure batch records and GMP compliance at all times.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm → production trainee at pharma company → production officer → senior officer → production manager

children.name: Quality Assurance (QA) and Regulatory Pharmacist
children.description: Audit manufacturing processes, prepare dossiers for DCGI, USFDA, or EU-GMP inspections, and maintain the quality management system at Indian pharma exporters.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm or M.Pharm → QA analyst at export-oriented pharma company → senior QA → regulatory affairs associate → RA manager

what_it_is: Manufacturing and ensuring quality of medicines at scale for global markets.
who_its_not_for: Those who want patient-facing clinical roles or research independence.
work_lifestyle: Factory floor and lab; shift-based; GMP-governed; documentation-heavy.
entry_route: B.Pharm then direct campus hire or M.Pharm for R&D-adjacent roles.
timeline: Analyst on graduation; senior analyst in 3 yrs; manager in 5–7 yrs.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹18L fresher to manager range at Indian generic pharma companies.
growth_and_progression: Analyst → senior analyst → QA manager → plant quality head.
demand_and_outlook: High; India exports medicines to 200+ countries and needs quality talent.
honest_caveat: Factory locations often in tier-2 or tier-3 cities; lifestyle trade-off real.

children.name: Hospital and Clinical Pharmacist
children.description: Work in hospital pharmacy departments counselling patients, managing drug inventory, and supporting clinical teams on medication safety and interactions.
children.salaryRange: ₹4L–₹12L / yr (corporate hospitals pay at higher end)
children.roadmap: B.Pharm → hospital internship → clinical pharmacist → senior pharmacist → pharmacy head at corporate hospital

what_it_is: Study and work with medicines from formulation to patient dispensing.
who_its_not_for: Those who want to prescribe; pharmacists dispense and counsel only.
work_lifestyle: Pharmacy counter, hospital ward, or manufacturing plant daily work.
entry_route: State pharmacy entrance or direct merit admission; no NEET required.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Pharm; M.Pharm 2 yrs for specialist or research roles.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹18L; pharma industry roles pay better than retail pharmacy.
growth_and_progression: Hospital pharmacist → clinical pharmacist → pharmacy manager or regulatory.
demand_and_outlook: High; India's pharma industry is third largest in the world by volume.
honest_caveat: Retail pharmacy salaries are low; industry or hospital roles are better paid.

children.name: BPT (Bachelor of Physiotherapy)
children.description: Four-year physiotherapy degree training students to rehabilitate patients with musculoskeletal, neurological, and sports injuries.

children.name: Sports and Orthopaedic Physiotherapist
children.description: Work with athletes, sports teams, and post-operative patients managing injuries and rehabilitation — the most visible and highest-paid physiotherapy niche.

children.name: Sports Team Physiotherapist
children.description: Travel with and treat IPL, ISL, or national sports team athletes — managing acute injuries, rehab, and performance conditioning throughout the season.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Sports → sports club junior physio → state team → IPL or national team physiotherapist

children.name: Orthopaedic Rehab Physiotherapist
children.description: Manage post-surgical rehabilitation for joint replacements, ACL repairs, and fractures at corporate hospitals or standalone orthopaedic clinics.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Orthopaedics → corporate hospital internship → orthopaedic physio → senior physiotherapist

what_it_is: Rehabilitating sports injuries and optimising athlete physical performance.
who_its_not_for: Those who want clinical variety beyond musculoskeletal conditions.
work_lifestyle: Pitch-side and clinic; travel with teams; irregular match-day hours.
entry_route: BPT then MPT Sports or Orthopaedics; sports club internship essential.
timeline: BPT 4.5 yrs + MPT 2 yrs; team physio role in 2–3 yrs post-MPT.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹25L / yr; IPL or national team contracts at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Team physiotherapist → chief physio → sports science director.
demand_and_outlook: Rising; India's sports ecosystem expanding with Olympics, IPL, and ISL.
honest_caveat: Team physio contracts competitive; own clinic is the safer income base.

children.name: Neurological Rehabilitation Physiotherapist
children.description: Rehabilitate stroke, spinal injury, cerebral palsy, and Parkinson's patients in hospital neuro units or dedicated rehab centres.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Neurology → neuro rehab internship → neurological physiotherapist at corporate or speciality hospital

what_it_is: Restore patient mobility and function through exercise and manual therapy.
who_its_not_for: Those who want desk work or lack physical empathy for patients.
work_lifestyle: Hands-on; patient-facing; hospital, sports club, or own clinic settings.
entry_route: State entrance or direct merit; some states use NEET score for ranking.
timeline: 4.5 yrs BPT including internship; MPT specialisation adds 2 yrs.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L employed; ₹10L–₹30L own practice in metro city.
growth_and_progression: Junior physiotherapist → senior → sports physio or neuro rehab specialist.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; sports boom, aging population, and post-COVID rehab driving demand.
honest_caveat: Hospital salaries are low; own practice or sports team contracts pay far better.

what_it_is: Pharmacy, physiotherapy, nursing, and paramedical science careers.
who_its_not_for: Those targeting MBBS or surgery; these are distinct clinical pathways.
work_lifestyle: Hospital, pharmacy, or clinic settings; patient interaction is daily work.
entry_route: State pharmacy entrance or direct admission; NEET for some programmes.
timeline: 4-yr B.Pharm or BPT; M.Pharm or MPT adds 2 more years.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L depending on role type and public vs private sector.
growth_and_progression: Clinical pharmacist, hospital manager, regulatory affairs, or own medical store.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's 10,500+ hospitals and 8 lakh+ pharmacies always hiring.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries lower than engineering; growth requires experience or PG study.

root.name: PCM
root.description: Career paths for In-School — PCM in India

children.name: Engineering & Technology
children.description: Core technical degrees built on maths and physics leading to India's highest-demand careers.

children.name: Computer Science Engineering (B.Tech CSE)
children.description: Four-year degree in software, systems, and computing — India's most in-demand engineering branch.

children.name: Software Development
children.description: Build products at tech companies ranging from IT services giants to FAANG-tier product firms.

children.name: SDE at Product Company (FAANG-tier or Unicorn)
children.description: Build and ship features at Google, Microsoft, Flipkart, Swiggy, or similar — highest compensation in Indian tech with significant stock grants.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → competitive programming Year 1 → FAANG-style internship → SDE-1 offer → SDE-2 in 2–3 yrs

children.name: SDE at IT Services (TCS / Infosys / Wipro / HCL)
children.description: Work on client delivery projects across BFSI, retail, and manufacturing. Easier campus entry; structured but lower pay than product companies.
children.salaryRange: ₹3.5L–₹8L / yr fresher; lateral moves increase this significantly
children.roadmap: B.Tech → on-campus placement → training programme → project allocation → upskill → move to product company

what_it_is: Writing, testing, and shipping software used by real users.
who_its_not_for: Those who want varied physical work or face-to-face client roles.
work_lifestyle: Sprint-based; pull requests; stand-ups; largely desk-bound work.
entry_route: DSA and system design prep; internships; campus or off-campus drives.
timeline: SDE-1 on graduation; SDE-2 in 2–3 yrs; senior in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹60L fresher depending on company tier and role type.
growth_and_progression: Staff engineer, engineering manager, or product manager track.
demand_and_outlook: Consistently highest demand role in Indian tech hiring market.
honest_caveat: Top pay concentrated at fewer than 50 companies in India.

children.name: AI and Machine Learning
children.description: Build intelligent systems — from recommendation engines to LLM-powered products — at India's fast-growing AI ecosystem.

children.name: Machine Learning Engineer (MLE)
children.description: Build, deploy, and monitor production ML models at tech, BFSI, or e-commerce companies — heavy focus on pipelines, feature stores, and model serving.
children.roadmap: B.Tech CSE → ML electives and Kaggle → ML intern → MLE role → senior MLE in 3–4 yrs

children.name: AI Research Engineer
children.description: Work at the frontier of model research at labs like Google DeepMind India, Microsoft Research, or academic institutes — publishing papers and building new architectures.
children.roadmap: B.Tech with strong math → research internship → M.Tech thesis or MS → research engineer → research scientist

what_it_is: Engineering production ML systems that solve real business problems.
who_its_not_for: Those uncomfortable with maths, statistics, or ambiguous problem spaces.
work_lifestyle: Mix of research, engineering, and stakeholder collaboration at pace.
entry_route: ML electives and Kaggle competitions; targeted ML internships in B.Tech.
timeline: MLE role on graduation; senior MLE in 3–4 yrs with strong execution.
salary_range: ₹12L–₹50L / yr; research labs and top product firms at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Senior MLE → ML architect → AI product manager or research lead.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every company building AI products actively needs ML engineers.
honest_caveat: Many AI roles are basic data work in practice; verify before accepting.

children.name: Cybersecurity
children.description: Protect networks, applications, and data — a fast-growing niche with a significant skill shortage across India's banking, tech, and government sectors.

children.name: Penetration Tester (Red Team)
children.description: Legally attack systems, networks, and applications to find vulnerabilities before malicious actors do — work at security consulting firms or enterprise red teams.
children.roadmap: B.Tech CSE → CTF competitions → OSCP or CEH cert → security intern → pen tester → senior red team engineer

children.name: Security Operations (SOC Analyst)
children.description: Monitor, detect, and respond to cyber threats in real time at security operations centres for banks, IT firms, or MSSPs serving Indian and global clients.
children.roadmap: B.Tech CSE → networking and Linux fundamentals → SOC analyst tier 1 → tier 2 → SOC lead or security engineer

what_it_is: Defending and ethically testing digital systems against real threats.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike deep technical rabbit holes or ambiguous challenges.
work_lifestyle: Proactive testing; monitoring; incident response; mostly desk-based.
entry_route: CSE B.Tech; CEH or OSCP certification helps; CTF participation essential.
timeline: Security analyst on graduation; engineer in 2–3 yrs; architect in 5+.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹40L; specialist skills command strong premium over generalist CS.
growth_and_progression: Security engineer → architect → CISO or independent consultant.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every bank and enterprise needs dedicated security talent.
honest_caveat: Skills must be continuously updated; certification treadmill is real.

what_it_is: Degree covering algorithms, systems, AI, and software engineering.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike screen-heavy, logic-intensive daily work.
work_lifestyle: Agile sprints; code reviews; largely remote-friendly across India.
entry_route: JEE Main or Advanced; state CET for local NIT or private college.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; placement or M.Tech or MS directly after.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹60L fresher CTC depending purely on company tier.
growth_and_progression: SDE → senior → staff engineer or product manager track.
demand_and_outlook: Extremely high; consistently the most-hired branch in India.
honest_caveat: Leetcode preparation from Year 1 is non-negotiable for top jobs.

children.name: Electronics and Communication Engineering (ECE)
children.description: Degree combining hardware, signals, embedded systems, and communications — gateway to semiconductors, telecom, and defence.

children.name: VLSI and Chip Design
children.description: Design processors, SoCs, and memory chips — the foundational skill for India's semiconductor ambitions.

children.name: RTL Design Engineer
children.description: Write and verify Verilog or VHDL code for digital logic blocks in CPUs, GPUs, or SoCs at Intel, Qualcomm, or MediaTek India design centres.
children.roadmap: B.Tech ECE → VLSI electives → M.Tech VLSI at IIT → RTL design engineer → senior engineer in 3–4 yrs

children.name: Verification Engineer (DV)
children.description: Verify chip designs using UVM testbenches and formal verification to guarantee correctness before costly silicon tapeout — extremely high demand niche.
children.roadmap: B.Tech ECE → UVM self-study and lab projects → DV intern → verification engineer → senior DV lead in 3–4 yrs

what_it_is: Designing the silicon inside every modern device and system.
who_its_not_for: Those who want fast iterations or consumer-facing product work.
work_lifestyle: EDA tools; simulation-heavy; long verification and tapeout cycles.
entry_route: VLSI electives in B.Tech; M.Tech at IIT or NIT strongly preferred.
timeline: B.Tech or M.Tech then hire; senior design engineer in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹35L; Intel, Qualcomm, MediaTek India at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Design engineer → senior → chip architect → principal engineer.
demand_and_outlook: Rising sharply; government semiconductor mission backing investment.
honest_caveat: M.Tech almost mandatory for design roles at top-tier semiconductor firms.

children.name: Embedded Systems and IoT
children.description: Program microcontrollers and build firmware for automobiles, smart appliances, industrial machines, and medical devices.
skills: I2C and SPI and UART protocols
children.roadmap: B.Tech ECE → hands-on Arduino or RPi projects → embedded intern → firmware engineer → senior embedded engineer

what_it_is: Bridges the physical and digital worlds from chips to 5G networks.
who_its_not_for: Those who want purely software careers from day one without hardware.
work_lifestyle: Lab and EDA tools mix; debug cycles slower than software iteration.
entry_route: JEE Main or Advanced for NITs; GATE for PSUs and M.Tech route.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; GATE or direct placement in final year.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹35L; VLSI and semiconductor firms pay at the top end.
growth_and_progression: Design engineer → lead → architect; or pivot into software roles.
demand_and_outlook: Rising fast; India Semiconductor Mission creating thousands of roles.
honest_caveat: Core ECE roles fewer than CS; VLSI specialisation or GATE strongly advised.

children.name: Mechanical Engineering
children.description: Design and manufacture physical systems — engines, EVs, robots, and structures — for India's industrial and automotive sectors.

children.name: EV and Automotive Engineering
children.description: Design battery packs, powertrains, and vehicle systems for India's rapidly growing electric vehicle industry.

children.name: EV Powertrain and Battery Engineer
children.description: Design battery management systems, motor controllers, and power electronics for EVs at Ola Electric, Tata Motors EV division, or Ather Energy.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Mech or EEE → EV-focused projects → intern at EV OEM → powertrain engineer → senior engineer in 3–4 yrs

children.name: Vehicle Dynamics and Structural Design Engineer
children.description: Design body structures, suspension geometry, and aerodynamic profiles using CAD and FEA tools at Mahindra, Tata, or Tier-1 auto suppliers.
children.roadmap: B.Tech Mech → CAD and FEA projects in college → intern at OEM → design engineer → senior design engineer

what_it_is: Engineering cars, two-wheelers, and EVs for Indian and global OEMs.
who_its_not_for: Those preferring digital-only or lab-only engineering environments.
work_lifestyle: CAD, FEA simulation, and test-track environment at OEM facilities.
entry_route: B.Tech Mech with EV electives; intern at Tata Motors or Ola Electric.
timeline: Graduate trainee on joining; design engineer in 2–3 years with focus.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹22L; EV startups can add equity on top of salary.
growth_and_progression: Engineer → systems lead → chief engineer → VP engineering.
demand_and_outlook: High and rising with India's EV push and PLI scheme for auto sector.
honest_caveat: Traditional OEM pace is slower than software; patent complexity high.

children.name: PSU via GATE (Mechanical)
children.description: Join BHEL, ONGC, NTPC, SAIL, or GAIL as a management trainee — a stable, well-perked government engineering career with pension and housing.

children.name: NTPC or BHEL Management Trainee (Mechanical)
children.description: Join NTPC Power or BHEL as a management trainee in operations, maintenance, or project departments — prestigious PSU career with full government benefits.
children.salaryRange: ₹9L–₹16L / yr CTC with housing and all allowances
children.roadmap: B.Tech Mech → GATE prep from Year 2 → score 700+ → NTPC or BHEL MT exam → joining → confirmed engineer in 1 yr

children.name: ONGC or GAIL Junior Engineer (Mechanical)
children.description: Work in oil and gas production, pipeline operations, or refinery maintenance at ONGC or GAIL across India's hydrocarbon assets.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹14L / yr CTC with remote area and HRA allowances
children.roadmap: B.Tech Mech → GATE score → ONGC or GAIL JE recruitment exam → technical and HR interview → field or plant posting

what_it_is: Government engineer roles at India's biggest public sector undertakings.
who_its_not_for: Those who want fast promotions, high salaries, or startup environments.
work_lifestyle: Plant and office mix; shift-based at some units; structured government pace.
entry_route: GATE Mechanical with strong score; PSU-specific cut-offs vary by company.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech → GATE prep → score 700+ → MT joining → confirmed in 1 yr.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹16L / yr CTC with housing, pension, medical, and allowances.
growth_and_progression: Management Trainee → Engineer → Senior Engineer → DGM → GM.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; BHEL, ONGC, NTPC recruit hundreds of Mech MTs annually.
honest_caveat: Promotions slow; PSU culture conservative; private pay often better.

what_it_is: Engineering of machines, thermal systems, and manufactured products.
who_its_not_for: Those wanting purely digital work with no physical prototyping.
work_lifestyle: CAD and simulation desk work plus shop-floor and testing lab time.
entry_route: JEE Main for NITs; state CETs; GATE for PSUs and M.Tech route.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Tech; PSU via GATE or private sector placement after.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L; PSU salary plus perks attractive over long term.
growth_and_progression: Design lead, plant manager, or MBA into operations and strategy.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; EV and aerospace sectors creating new openings now.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries lower than CS; MBA or GATE needed to accelerate.

what_it_is: B.Tech and B.E. degrees training engineers for industry and research.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike logic, maths, or prolonged problem-solving.
work_lifestyle: Desk and lab mix; collaborative teams; fast technology change cycles.
entry_route: JEE Main or Advanced for NITs and IITs; state CETs for local colleges.
timeline: 4-year B.Tech; job or PG immediately after graduation.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹60L+ depending on branch, college tier, and company.
growth_and_progression: Senior engineer, architect, manager, or entrepreneur over 5–10 yrs.
demand_and_outlook: Very high across CS and ECE; moderate in Mech and Civil.
honest_caveat: College tier shapes first-job heavily; branch choice matters less later.

children.name: Pure Sciences and Research
children.description: Rigorous science degrees for students driven by curiosity about how the universe works.

children.name: B.Sc in Physics, Chemistry, or Mathematics
children.description: Three-year foundational science degree leading to government research, teaching, or industry pivot via postgraduate study.

children.name: Research Scientist via PhD
children.description: Conduct original research at IISc, TIFR, DRDO, or CSIR national labs — India's most prestigious science career path.

children.name: CSIR or TIFR Research Scientist
children.description: Join a CSIR lab (NCL, CCMB, IICB) or TIFR as a scientist after PhD — fundamental research in chemistry, biology, or physics with full government security and pension.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹18L / yr Scientist B with all allowances
children.roadmap: B.Sc → M.Sc → CSIR-NET JRF → PhD at CSIR lab → post-doc → Scientist B via direct recruitment

children.name: University Faculty (Lecturer or Assistant Professor)
children.description: Join a central or state university as assistant professor after PhD — teach undergraduates, run a research group, and apply for DST or SERB grants.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹18L / yr UGC 7th Pay Commission academic scale
children.roadmap: B.Sc → M.Sc → NET or PhD → assistant professor recruitment → research group → associate and full professor

what_it_is: Original scientific discovery at government or premier institute labs.
who_its_not_for: Those who need external milestones or quick income validation.
work_lifestyle: Lab-intensive; grant and publication cycles; semi-independent work.
entry_route: B.Sc then M.Sc; CSIR-NET or GATE for PhD fellowship funding.
timeline: B.Sc 3 + M.Sc 2 + PhD 5 = at least 10 years to scientist post.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹7L JRF during PhD; ₹10L–₹20L as scientist post-PhD.
growth_and_progression: Scientist B → C → principal scientist → division head.
demand_and_outlook: Steady but seats limited; CSIR, TIFR, BARC hire hundreds annually.
honest_caveat: Post-doc years abroad increasingly expected for IIT faculty positions.

children.name: Data Science via Pure Mathematics
children.description: Leverage strong mathematical foundations to pivot into ML, quant research, or analytics — a realistic industry route without a CS engineering degree.

children.name: Data Scientist at Tech or BFSI Company
children.description: Build predictive models and dashboards for product, operations, or risk teams at tech startups or banks and insurance companies across India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Maths → online ML and SQL courses → DS internship → data scientist → senior DS in 3–4 yrs

children.name: Quant Analyst at Prop Trading or Hedge Fund
children.description: Build systematic trading strategies and risk models at prop trading firms like Graviton, Tower Research, or Optiver India — the best-paid pure-maths career in India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or IISER Maths → quant olympiad or competitive maths → quant research intern → junior quant → senior researcher

what_it_is: Applying maths and statistics to solve data-driven business problems.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike coding or abstract statistical reasoning.
work_lifestyle: Mostly desk and laptop; stakeholder-facing; notebook and pipeline work.
entry_route: B.Sc Maths then M.Sc Stats or direct DS internship with online upskilling.
timeline: M.Sc or certifications in 2 yrs; DS role; senior in 3–4 yrs after that.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹30L / yr depending on company and domain applied to.
growth_and_progression: DS → senior DS → lead DS → head of analytics or ML engineering.
demand_and_outlook: High; strong maths background differentiates from average CS graduates.
honest_caveat: Must actively learn Python and ML tools; maths alone is insufficient.

what_it_is: Deep subject mastery in one or two core sciences over three years.
who_its_not_for: Those who want immediate industry employment after their first degree.
work_lifestyle: Lecture, tutorial, and lab cycles; self-study and exam-driven.
entry_route: CUET for Delhi University and central colleges; state entrances.
timeline: 3 yrs B.Sc; M.Sc 2 yrs; optional PhD another 5 yrs after that.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹8L after B.Sc alone; much higher post-M.Sc or PhD.
growth_and_progression: Teacher, government scientist, or industry pivot via M.Sc.
demand_and_outlook: Limited direct; government lab seats and faculty posts are scarce.
honest_caveat: Without M.Sc or PhD, career options and salaries are very restricted.

children.name: Integrated M.Sc at IISERs or IISc
children.description: Five-year elite research degree at India's premier science institutes — the best pure-science undergraduate track in the country.

children.name: PhD and Faculty Career
children.description: Pursue a doctorate at IISc, TIFR, or abroad and build a career in research and university teaching at India's top institutes.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹7L JRF fellowship; ₹14L–₹22L as IIT assistant professor
children.roadmap: IISER BS-MS → PhD India or abroad → post-doc 1–3 yrs → assistant professor at IIT or IISc → research group

children.name: Quant Analyst or Industry Scientist
children.description: Pivot from an IISER degree into quant finance, data science, or industrial R&D at pharma, fintech, or tech companies.
children.roadmap: IISER BS-MS → industry internship in Year 4–5 → quant analyst or DS role → senior analyst or scientist

what_it_is: Premier BS-MS programme blending undergraduate and postgraduate study.
who_its_not_for: Those targeting engineering placements or management careers primarily.
work_lifestyle: Deeply academic; research rotations from Year 3; seminar-driven pace.
entry_route: IISER Aptitude Test (IAT), JEE Advanced channel, or KVPY score.
timeline: 5 years integrated; then PhD 5 yrs or direct industry entry.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹25L in industry; significantly higher post-PhD in quant or ML.
growth_and_progression: Faculty, national lab scientist, or high-skill industry pivot.
demand_and_outlook: Very small intake; alumni highly valued in research and quant sectors.
honest_caveat: Primarily leads to academia; industry pivot requires strong self-initiative.

what_it_is: B.Sc and integrated MS degrees in physics, chemistry, or maths.
who_its_not_for: Those who need high income or fast career milestones after graduation.
work_lifestyle: Seminar and lab-heavy; self-directed; publication and fellowship cycles.
entry_route: CUET for central universities; IAT or KVPY or JEE for IISERs and IISc.
timeline: 3-yr B.Sc or 5-yr integrated MS; PhD adds another 5 years after.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹10L after first degree; ₹8L–₹40L post-PhD in right domain.
growth_and_progression: Research scientist, professor, or industry pivot to DS and quant roles.
demand_and_outlook: Niche; government labs and premier universities are primary employers.
honest_caveat: Very long runway; PhD nearly mandatory for sustainable research career.

children.name: Defence and Government Services
children.description: Armed forces, civil services, and defence research careers combining service, stability, and national impact.

children.name: Indian Armed Forces (Technical Entry)
children.description: Officer-level entry into Army, Navy, or Air Force via PCM-based technical schemes directly from Class 12.

children.name: Army Technical Entry Scheme (TES)
children.description: Join the Corps of Engineers, EME, or Signals as a technical officer straight from Class 12 PCM — SSB interview only, no written exam required.
children.salaryRange: ₹9L–₹15L / yr Lieutenant CTC including all allowances
children.roadmap: Class 12 PCM 70%+ → TES notification → SSB Service Selection Board → 4-yr technical training at CME Pune → commissioned officer

children.name: NDA (Air Force or Navy Technical)
children.description: Join the National Defence Academy after Class 12 and choose a technical branch — pilot, ATC officer, or naval engineer — with a structured full officer career.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹18L / yr with flying or technical branch allowances
children.roadmap: Class 12 → NDA written exam (UPSC) → SSB → 3 yrs NDA Khadakwasla → service academy → commissioned officer

what_it_is: Commissioned officer roles in India's military technical branches.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to face frequent transfers or rigid discipline codes.
work_lifestyle: Physical and technical; postings across India including border areas.
entry_route: TES for Army; INCET for Navy; NDA or AFCAT for Air Force.
timeline: Selection and training 1–1.5 yrs; commissioned as Lieutenant.
salary_range: ₹9L–₹18L / yr CTC with all allowances and perks included.
growth_and_progression: Lieutenant → Major → Colonel → Brigadier; strong PSU exit options.
demand_and_outlook: Steady annual intake with rigorous physical and psychological screening.
honest_caveat: Frequent transfers significantly affect personal and family stability.

children.name: UPSC Civil Services
children.description: India's most prestigious government career — IAS, IPS, or IFS — powered by the analytical strength built through PCM.

children.name: IAS Officer (Indian Administrative Service)
children.description: Administer districts and lead state and central government departments — the most powerful generalist career in India.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹20L / yr 7th Pay Commission with housing, transport, and security
children.roadmap: Graduation → UPSC prep 1–3 yrs → Prelims → Mains → Personality Test → LBSNAA Mussoorie training → SDM posting

children.name: IPS Officer (Indian Police Service)
children.description: Lead law enforcement from Assistant Superintendent to Director General of Police — managing crime, intelligence, and public order across a 30-year career.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹18L / yr with government accommodation and security
children.roadmap: Graduation → UPSC CSE → IPS allotment → SVP NPA Hyderabad training → ASP posting in cadre state

what_it_is: Top administrative, police, or diplomatic career in the Indian government.
who_its_not_for: Those who need high pay, stability of location, or fast promotions.
work_lifestyle: Highly varied; district administration, policy, and field postings mixed.
entry_route: Any graduation first; then UPSC CSE Prelims → Mains → Interview.
timeline: Graduation plus 1–3 years dedicated preparation for most candidates.
salary_range: ₹10L–₹20L pay plus allowances; influence and power exceed salary.
growth_and_progression: SDM → District Collector → Joint Secretary → Secretary to Government.
demand_and_outlook: About 900 vacancies per year; over 10 lakh applicants every cycle.
honest_caveat: 2–4 year opportunity cost of preparation; success rate under 0.2%.

children.name: DRDO (Defence Research and Development Organisation)
children.description: Work on India's strategic defence technology — missiles, radar, electronics, and cyber defence — as a government scientist after B.Tech.

children.name: DRDO Scientist — Electronics and CS
children.description: Work on radar, avionics, communication systems, and cyber defence at DRDO labs like LRDE Bengaluru or CAIR — strategic and mission-critical work.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹18L / yr Scientist B to D scale
children.roadmap: B.Tech ECE or CSE → DRDO CEPTAM or SET exam → Scientist B → lab project posting → Scientist C on performance review

children.name: DRDO Scientist — Mechanical and Aerospace
children.description: Contribute to missile airframe design, propulsion, and armoured vehicle development at DRDL Hyderabad, CVRDE Chennai, or TBRL Chandigarh.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹18L / yr Scientist B to D scale
children.roadmap: B.Tech Mech or Aerospace → DRDO SET exam → Scientist B joining → specialised project lab → Scientist C

what_it_is: R&D on India's defence systems including Agni, Akash, and Tejas.
who_its_not_for: Those who want commercial salaries, publications, or fast promotions.
work_lifestyle: Lab and field testing; classified projects; structured government pace.
entry_route: DRDO CEPTAM exam or SET exam for Scientist B after B.Tech.
timeline: B.Tech then DRDO exam; Scientist B joining; promotion every 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹18L / yr 7th Pay Commission with research and project allowances.
growth_and_progression: Scientist B → C → D → E → Distinguished Scientist over 20+ yrs.
demand_and_outlook: Limited seats; ECE, CSE, and Mech streams always in active recruitment.
honest_caveat: Classified work limits publications and external industry visibility.

what_it_is: Military officer, IAS, or defence scientist roles for PCM graduates.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to relocate frequently or accept structured hierarchies.
work_lifestyle: Disciplined; structured posting cycles; mix of field and desk work.
entry_route: NDA or TES after Class 12; UPSC after graduation; DRDO after B.Tech.
timeline: 1–4 yrs training after selection; long service career follows after.
salary_range: ₹9L–₹20L with allowances; non-cash perks add substantial real value.
growth_and_progression: Rank progression in forces; Secretary level in civil services.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; hundreds of vacancies announced every year across services.
honest_caveat: Selection is intensely competitive; personal and family sacrifices are real.

children.name: Architecture and Spatial Design
children.description: Creative and technical degree paths for PCM students with spatial thinking and design sensibility.

children.name: B.Arch (Bachelor of Architecture)
children.description: Five-year professional degree in building design, urban space, and architectural technology.

children.name: Architectural Designer at Practice
children.description: Work in private or government architecture firms designing residential, commercial, institutional, and hospitality projects across India.
children.roadmap: NATA → B.Arch 5 yrs → COA registration → junior architect at firm → 5+ yrs experience → project architect → own practice

children.name: Urban Planner and Sustainable Designer
children.description: Plan cities, townships, transit corridors, and public spaces — increasingly central to India's Smart Cities Mission and AMRUT programme.
children.roadmap: B.Arch → M.Plan at IIT or SPA New Delhi → urban planner at DUDA or consulting firm → senior planner → project director

what_it_is: Design buildings and spaces from concept through construction drawings.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike hand-drawing, long studio hours, or site visits.
work_lifestyle: Studio deadlines; site visits; client presentations; design reviews.
entry_route: NATA score and JEE Paper 2 B.Arch; Maths in Class 12 required.
timeline: 5 yrs B.Arch; COA registration; practice-ready in Year 6–7 typically.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹18L employed; ₹15L–₹50L+ own practice after 8–10 yrs.
growth_and_progression: Junior architect → project architect → principal or own firm.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; smart cities, affordable housing, and commercial real estate demand.
honest_caveat: Long road to meaningful income; junior years are often poorly paid.

children.name: Interior Design and Spatial Technology
children.description: Degree and diploma paths in interior design and computational design tools for students who blend technical and aesthetic thinking.

children.name: Interior and Spatial Designer
children.description: Design residential, commercial, hospitality, and retail interiors — combining aesthetics, materials, lighting, and ergonomics for end-users.
children.roadmap: B.Arch or B.Des Interior → intern at interior design firm → junior designer → senior designer → own studio in 6–8 yrs

children.name: Computational and Parametric Design
children.description: Use Grasshopper, Rhino, and generative AI tools to design complex architectural forms — a niche but globally sought-after specialisation for digitally minded architects.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹28L / yr (higher at global firms and research studios)
children.roadmap: B.Arch → M.Arch Computational Design at CEPT or abroad → parametric design role at advanced architecture studio → design lead

what_it_is: Designing interior environments, retail spaces, and hospitality projects.
who_its_not_for: Those who want heavy structural engineering or outdoor civil work.
work_lifestyle: Studio and client-site mix; material sourcing; vendor management.
entry_route: NID entrance or CEED for M.Des; state design college entrances for B.Des.
timeline: 4-yr B.Des or 2-yr M.Des after B.Arch; practice-ready in 1–2 yrs after.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L; own studio or luxury residential projects pay significantly more.
growth_and_progression: Junior designer → senior designer → design director → own studio.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; premium real estate, hospitality, and retail fit-out booming.
honest_caveat: Client payments can be irregular; own studio requires business skills.

what_it_is: B.Arch and planning degrees designing buildings, cities, and environments.
who_its_not_for: Those with no interest in aesthetics, drawing, or spatial problem-solving.
work_lifestyle: Studio culture; project deadlines; mix of creative and technical work.
entry_route: NATA score plus JEE Paper 2 for B.Arch; Class 12 Maths mandatory.
timeline: 5 yrs B.Arch; COA registration after; M.Arch adds 2 more years.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹25L; experience and firm reputation drive earnings more than degree.
growth_and_progression: Junior architect → project architect → senior architect → own practice.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's real estate and infrastructure boom creating long-term demand.
honest_caveat: Entry salaries low; income builds slowly and unevenly over years.

root.name: PCB
root.description: Career paths for In-School — PCB in India

children.name: Medicine and Clinical Sciences
children.description: Patient-care degrees for PCB students — India's most socially respected and demand-proof career cluster.

children.name: MBBS (Allopathy)
children.description: India's primary five-and-a-half year medical degree — the gold standard clinical career gateway.

children.name: Clinical Specialist via MD or MS
children.description: Pursue NEET-PG after internship to specialise in one of 20+ clinical branches — the path to high income and professional identity.

children.name: General Surgeon (MS Surgery)
children.description: Perform abdominal, laparoscopic, and emergency surgeries at government or private hospitals — one of the most respected clinical specialities in India.
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → MBBS 5.5 yrs → internship → NEET-PG → MS Surgery 3 yrs → senior residency → consultant surgeon

children.name: Physician or Internal Medicine Specialist (MD)
children.description: Diagnose and manage complex medical conditions — diabetes, cardiac, respiratory — as a physician at hospitals or in private practice across India.
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → MBBS → NEET-PG → MD Internal Medicine 3 yrs → physician or DM super-speciality → consultant

what_it_is: Postgraduate clinical specialisation defining your medical identity forever.
who_its_not_for: Those who cannot endure another 3 years of intense residency pressure.
work_lifestyle: Residency is gruelling; post-qualification practice is rewarding and independent.
entry_route: NEET-PG after completing MBBS internship; All India rank determines branch.
timeline: 3 years MD or MS; then fellowship or direct independent practice.
salary_range: ₹20L–₹80L / yr depending on speciality and city tier.
growth_and_progression: Consultant → senior consultant → department head → own hospital.
demand_and_outlook: High; surgical and procedural specialities always oversubscribed with patients.
honest_caveat: Government PG seats very scarce; rural bond service often compulsory.

children.name: Super-Speciality via DM or MCh
children.description: After MD or MS, pursue a 3-year DM or MCh super-speciality for the highest clinical prestige and income in all of Indian medicine.
children.salaryRange: ₹40L–₹2Cr / yr (interventional cardiologists and neurosurgeons at top)
children.roadmap: MBBS → MD or MS → NEET-SS → DM or MCh 3 yrs → super-specialist practice → own hospital or senior position

what_it_is: Train to become a fully NMC-licensed medical doctor in India.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to invest 10+ years before peak earnings begin.
work_lifestyle: Ward rounds, OPD, on-call nights, and emergency duties throughout.
entry_route: NEET-UG; state and All India quota; 720-mark exam once per year.
timeline: MBBS 5.5 yrs; internship included; then NEET-PG for specialisation.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹20L government; ₹20L–₹80L private specialist in practice.
growth_and_progression: Registrar → consultant → senior consultant → department head.
demand_and_outlook: Always high; every district hospital and clinic needs more doctors.
honest_caveat: Only 90,000 MBBS seats nationally against over 20 lakh NEET takers.

children.name: BDS (Bachelor of Dental Surgery)
children.description: Five-year dental degree — lower NEET cutoff than MBBS, strong own-practice income potential, and a clearly defined clinical career.

children.name: General Dentist and Clinic Owner
children.description: Build an independent dental clinic after DCI registration — the most common and financially rewarding long-term dental career in India.

children.name: Urban General Dental Practitioner
children.description: Practise general dentistry — fillings, extractions, crowns, and basic orthodontics — at a standalone or polyclinic setup in a metro or tier-1 city.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹30L / yr (associate to own metro clinic range)
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → BDS 5 yrs → internship → DCI registration → associate dentist → own clinic in 5–8 yrs

children.name: Dental Surgeon at Government Hospital
children.description: Work in a district or taluk government hospital as a dental surgeon — stable salaried career with pension, housing, and no capital investment required.
children.roadmap: BDS → internship → state dental services recruitment exam → government dental surgeon → senior dental surgeon

what_it_is: Running a dental practice treating all ages in a defined catchment area.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike the business side of healthcare or capital investment.
work_lifestyle: Clinic-based; appointment-driven; own hours once established.
entry_route: BDS → internship → DCI registration → associate role → own clinic.
timeline: Own clinic viable in 5–8 yrs; associate income from Year 1 after BDS.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹50L / yr (associate to established metro clinic range).
growth_and_progression: General dentist → multi-chair clinic → dental chain or franchise.
demand_and_outlook: Rising; urban dental awareness and insurance penetration growing fast.
honest_caveat: Prime location and equipment cost ₹20–₹40L upfront; ROI takes years.

children.name: Dental Specialist via MDS
children.description: Specialise in orthodontics, oral surgery, periodontics, or endodontics via NEET-MDS for premium metro clinical income and academic opportunities.
children.salaryRange: ₹15L–₹60L / yr specialist in metro or tier-1 city
children.roadmap: BDS → internship → NEET-MDS preparation → MDS 3 yrs → specialist practice or dental college teaching post

what_it_is: Train as a licensed dentist with surgical and clinical treatment skills.
who_its_not_for: Those seeking large-hospital medicine or research-driven careers.
work_lifestyle: Clinic-based; hands-on procedures; own practice is the long-term goal.
entry_route: NEET-UG; government BDS seats require roughly 450–500 for general.
timeline: 5 yrs BDS including internship; MDS specialisation adds 3 more years.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L employed; ₹15L–₹50L established own clinic in 5–8 yrs.
growth_and_progression: General dentist → MDS specialist → clinic chain or dental college faculty.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; dental insurance penetration and awareness rising across India.
honest_caveat: Own practice requires significant capital and a good patient-footfall location.

children.name: BAMS or BHMS (AYUSH Degrees)
children.description: Five-and-a-half year Ayurveda or Homeopathy degrees accessible via NEET-UG with lower cutoff — government-recognised with own-practice potential.

children.name: AYUSH Government Medical Officer
children.description: Join a state government AYUSH dispensary or hospital as a medical officer — stable salaried career with pension and no capital risk of private practice.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹14L / yr state government AYUSH MO scale
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → BAMS or BHMS 5.5 yrs → internship → state AYUSH recruitment exam → MO at government dispensary → senior MO

children.name: Ayurveda Wellness and Panchkarma Practitioner
children.description: Run an Ayurveda clinic or wellness centre offering classical treatments and Panchkarma — high urban and international demand for authentic practitioners.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹25L / yr (metro wellness centre at higher end)
children.roadmap: BAMS 5.5 yrs → internship → CCIM registration → associate at Ayurveda hospital → own clinic or wellness centre

what_it_is: Traditional medicine degrees with AYUSH council recognition and scope.
who_its_not_for: Those who want allopathic prescription rights or hospital-based careers.
work_lifestyle: Clinic and wellness centre practice; less hospital rotation than MBBS.
entry_route: NEET-UG; substantially lower cutoff than MBBS; state AYUSH quota seats.
timeline: 5.5 yrs including internship; PG via AYUSH PG entrance exam.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L employed; ₹10L–₹30L established own practice.
growth_and_progression: General practitioner → speciality clinic → wellness chain or herbal brand.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; government AYUSH hospitals and wellness tourism both growing.
honest_caveat: Prescription scope narrower than MBBS; urban patient acceptance still building.

what_it_is: Clinical degrees training students to diagnose, treat, and heal patients.
who_its_not_for: Those averse to blood, death, emotional burden, or decade-long study.
work_lifestyle: Hospital rotations; long shifts; high responsibility; emotionally intense.
entry_route: NEET-UG is the single gateway for all clinical degrees in India.
timeline: MBBS 5.5 yrs; MD or MS adds 3 more; super-speciality 3 more after.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹2Cr depending on specialisation, city, and practice type.
growth_and_progression: General doctor → specialist → super-specialist → hospital founder.
demand_and_outlook: Perennially high; India has roughly one doctor per 1,500 people still.
honest_caveat: Government MBBS seat requires 600+ NEET; private fees reach ₹1.5Cr.

children.name: Life Sciences, Biotech, and Research
children.description: Biology-driven degree paths leading to pharma, biotech, genomics, and research careers.

children.name: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotechnology
children.description: Three or four-year degree combining biology, chemistry, and engineering for pharma, biopharma, and biotech industry careers.

children.name: Pharma Research and Drug Development
children.description: Work on drug discovery, formulation, or process development at Indian pharma majors like Dr Reddy's, Sun Pharma, or Cipla.

children.name: Drug Discovery Scientist
children.description: Identify and optimise new drug candidates through biological assays, medicinal chemistry, and screening at pharma companies like Dr Reddy's or Biocon.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech → M.Sc Pharmaceutical Sciences → research associate → scientist → senior scientist in 5–7 yrs

children.name: Formulation Development Scientist
children.description: Develop oral solid dosage forms, injectables, and biologics formulations for India's massive generic drug export industry — core to Sun Pharma and Cipla operations.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm or B.Tech Biotech → M.Pharm Pharmaceutics → formulation scientist → senior formulation scientist in 4–6 yrs

what_it_is: Discovering and developing new medicines for global markets.
who_its_not_for: Those who need fast results or external validation through visible products.
work_lifestyle: Lab bench; GMP-governed; long experiment cycles; publication-driven.
entry_route: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech then M.Sc Pharma or direct research associate hire.
timeline: Research associate on M.Sc; scientist in 3–4 yrs; senior in 6–8.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹25L fresher to senior scientist range at Indian pharma.
growth_and_progression: Scientist → principal scientist → R&D project leader → R&D head.
demand_and_outlook: High; India files more global drug patents than any country except the USA.
honest_caveat: PhD strongly preferred for senior scientist or director-level positions.

children.name: Bioinformatics and Genomics
children.description: Analyse genome, proteome, and drug-target data using programming — the fastest-growing intersection of biology and data science in India and globally.

children.name: Genomics Data Scientist
children.description: Analyse whole-genome sequencing and RNA-seq data at genomics startups like MedGenome, research hospitals, or CSIR-IGIB in India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Bio → M.Sc Bioinformatics at NCBS or Hyderabad → genomics internship → data scientist at genomics firm

children.name: Computational Drug Discovery Scientist
children.description: Use molecular docking, MD simulation, and ML to identify drug candidates — a high-value role at biopharma companies and AI-drug startups in India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc or B.Tech Biotech → M.Sc Bioinformatics → computational chem internship → drug discovery scientist at biotech or AI-pharma startup

what_it_is: Using code and statistics to decode massive biological datasets.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike programming or abstract computational problem-solving.
work_lifestyle: Mostly computational; collaborative with wet lab teams; notebook-driven.
entry_route: B.Sc Bio then M.Sc Bioinformatics; Python self-learning essential alongside.
timeline: M.Sc 2 yrs; bioinformatics role; senior analyst in 3–4 yrs after.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹35L; genomics and AI drug discovery pay at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Bioinformatics scientist → senior → computational biology lead.
demand_and_outlook: Rising sharply; genomics, personalised medicine, and AI drug discovery booming.
honest_caveat: Must genuinely learn to code; biology alone will not get you here.

what_it_is: Applying biological knowledge to make drugs, vaccines, and diagnostics.
who_its_not_for: Those who want immediate industry roles without postgraduate preparation.
work_lifestyle: Lab bench; GMP-governed; regulatory documentation part of every day.
entry_route: CUET for B.Sc; JEE Main for B.Tech Biotech at NITs and private colleges.
timeline: 3–4 yr degree; M.Sc or M.Tech adds 2 yrs; PhD another 5 after that.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹12L after first degree; much higher post-M.Sc or PhD.
growth_and_progression: Research associate → scientist → principal scientist → R&D project lead.
demand_and_outlook: Good; Serum Institute, Biocon, Bharat Biotech always hiring biology talent.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries low; postgraduate study almost mandatory for progression.

children.name: B.Sc in Zoology, Botany, or Microbiology
children.description: Pure biology degree leading to government service, teaching, or specialised research via postgraduate study.

children.name: Government Scientist or Conservation Officer
children.description: Join ICAR, ICMR, or state forest departments as a scientific officer or forest ranger after qualifying CSIR-NET or forest services exam.

children.name: ICAR Agricultural or Animal Sciences Scientist
children.description: Conduct crop improvement, animal husbandry, or fisheries research at ICAR institutes spread across India — mission-driven government science with full benefits.
children.salaryRange: ₹7L–₹15L / yr ICAR scientist scale with allowances
children.roadmap: B.Sc Bio or Agriculture → M.Sc → ICAR-ARS NET → scientist posting at ICAR institute → senior scientist

children.name: Indian Forest Service (IFS) Officer
children.description: Manage India's 7.12 lakh sq km of forest land — biodiversity conservation, wildlife protection, and tribal welfare — as a gazetted IFS officer via UPSC.
children.salaryRange: ₹10L–₹18L / yr 7th Pay Commission with forest allowances and housing
children.roadmap: B.Sc or M.Sc Biology → UPSC Civil Services with IFS option → IGNFA Dehradun training → DFO posting

what_it_is: Science or conservation work in government labs and forest departments.
who_its_not_for: Those who want high commercial salaries or metro-based desk careers.
work_lifestyle: Field and lab mix; government pace; structured posting cycles.
entry_route: M.Sc then CSIR-NET or UPSC IFS for Indian Forest Service.
timeline: B.Sc 3 + M.Sc 2 + exam prep 1 yr; long but very stable runway.
salary_range: ₹6L–₹18L / yr government scientist or forest officer scale.
