growth_and_progression: Scientific officer → senior scientist → principal scientist → division head.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; ICAR, ICMR, and state forest services recruit every year.
honest_caveat: Competitive exams required; promotion timelines are slow in government.

children.name: Quality Control and Food Safety Analyst
children.description: Test food, pharma, and consumer goods for safety and regulatory compliance at FSSAI-registered labs and manufacturing companies across India.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Micro or Chemistry → M.Sc Food Technology or Microbiology → QC trainee → analyst → QC manager in 5–7 yrs

what_it_is: Foundational biology covering living organisms and ecological systems.
who_its_not_for: Those targeting engineering, medicine, or immediate industry-facing roles.
work_lifestyle: Field surveys, lab work, and academic study at a slower, deliberate pace.
entry_route: CUET for central universities; state entrance exams for state colleges.
timeline: 3 yr B.Sc; M.Sc 2 yrs; PhD another 5 yrs for research career.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹8L after B.Sc; ₹8L–₹20L post-M.Sc in government or industry.
growth_and_progression: Government scientist, conservation officer, teacher, or quality analyst.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; government labs, forest departments, and food industry hire.
honest_caveat: Very limited industry roles after B.Sc alone; M.Sc is near-mandatory.

what_it_is: B.Sc and B.Tech degrees applying biological science to industry and discovery.
who_its_not_for: Those who need high income immediately after their first degree.
work_lifestyle: Lab-intensive; project-based; documentation and publication cycles govern pace.
entry_route: CUET for central universities; IISER IAT or KVPY for premier institutes.
timeline: 3-yr B.Sc or 4-yr B.Tech; M.Sc or PhD adds 2–5 more years after.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L after first degree; ₹12L–₹40L post-M.Sc or PhD in industry.
growth_and_progression: Research associate → scientist → principal scientist → R&D head or founder.
demand_and_outlook: Growing fast; India's pharma, vaccine, and genomics sectors all expanding.
honest_caveat: Pure B.Sc alone limits options significantly; M.Sc or specialisation is essential.

children.name: Allied Health and Pharmacy Sciences
children.description: Clinical and pharmaceutical degree paths accessible via NEET or direct state entrances — shorter than MBBS with strong demand.

children.name: B.Pharm (Bachelor of Pharmacy)
children.description: Four-year pharmacy degree covering drug formulation, pharmacology, and quality assurance — gateway to pharma industry and hospital pharmacy careers.

children.name: Pharma Industry (Production and QA)
children.description: Work in drug manufacturing, quality assurance, or regulatory affairs at Indian pharma companies — the well-paying side of a pharmacy degree.

children.name: Production Pharmacist
children.description: Oversee manufacturing of tablets, capsules, and injectables at pharma plants — ensuring batch records, GMP compliance, and yield targets are met every shift.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm → production trainee → production officer → senior officer → production manager in 5–7 yrs

children.name: Quality Assurance and Regulatory Pharmacist
children.description: Prepare USFDA and EU-GMP dossiers, audit manufacturing processes, and handle CAPA at India's export-oriented pharma companies — premium-paying niche.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm or M.Pharm → QA analyst at export pharma → senior QA → regulatory affairs associate → RA manager

what_it_is: Manufacturing and quality-assuring medicines for Indian and export markets.
who_its_not_for: Those who want patient-facing clinical roles or research independence.
work_lifestyle: Factory floor and lab; shift-based; GMP-governed; documentation-heavy.
entry_route: B.Pharm direct campus hire; M.Pharm for R&D-adjacent roles preferred.
timeline: Analyst on graduation; senior analyst in 3 yrs; manager in 5–7 yrs.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹18L fresher to manager at Indian generic pharma companies.
growth_and_progression: Analyst → senior → QA manager → plant quality head → site director.
demand_and_outlook: High; India exports medicines to 200+ countries needing quality talent.
honest_caveat: Factory locations often tier-2 or tier-3 cities; lifestyle trade-off real.

children.name: Clinical and Hospital Pharmacist
children.description: Counsel patients, manage drug inventory, and support clinical teams on medication safety at corporate hospitals across India.
children.roadmap: B.Pharm → hospital internship → clinical pharmacist → senior pharmacist → head of pharmacy at corporate hospital

what_it_is: Study and work with medicines from molecule to patient dispensing.
who_its_not_for: Those who want to prescribe; pharmacists counsel and dispense only.
work_lifestyle: Pharmacy counter, hospital ward, or manufacturing plant daily work.
entry_route: State pharmacy entrance or direct merit; no NEET required for most.
timeline: 4 yrs B.Pharm; M.Pharm 2 yrs for specialist or research roles.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹18L; pharma industry roles pay much better than retail pharmacy.
growth_and_progression: Hospital pharmacist → clinical → pharmacy manager → regulatory affairs.
demand_and_outlook: High; India's pharma industry is third largest globally by volume.
honest_caveat: Retail pharmacy salaries are low; industry or hospital roles far better.

children.name: BPT (Bachelor of Physiotherapy)
children.description: Four-and-a-half year physiotherapy degree training students to rehabilitate patients with musculoskeletal, neurological, and sports injuries.

children.name: Sports and Orthopaedic Physiotherapy
children.description: Work with athletes and post-surgical orthopaedic patients — the highest-paid and most visible physiotherapy niche in India's growing sports ecosystem.

children.name: Sports Team Physiotherapist
children.description: Travel and work with IPL, ISL, or national sports team athletes — managing acute injuries, season-long rehab, and performance conditioning on and off the pitch.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Sports → sports club junior physio → state team → IPL or national team physiotherapist contract

children.name: Orthopaedic Rehabilitation Physiotherapist
children.description: Manage post-surgical rehab for joint replacements, ACL repairs, and fractures at corporate hospitals or standalone orthopaedic clinics across metro India.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Orthopaedics → corporate hospital internship → orthopaedic physio → senior physiotherapist in 3–4 yrs

what_it_is: Rehabilitating sports injuries and restoring athlete physical performance.
who_its_not_for: Those who want clinical variety beyond musculoskeletal conditions.
work_lifestyle: Pitch-side and clinic; team travel; match-day irregular hours.
entry_route: BPT then MPT Sports or Orthopaedics; sports club internship essential.
timeline: BPT 4.5 yrs + MPT 2 yrs; team physio role possible in 2–3 yrs after.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹25L / yr; IPL or national team contracts at higher end.
growth_and_progression: Team physio → chief physiotherapist → sports science director.
demand_and_outlook: Rising; India's IPL, ISL, Pro Kabaddi, and Olympic ecosystem expanding.
honest_caveat: Team physio contracts competitive; own clinic is the safer income base.

children.name: Neurological Rehabilitation Physiotherapist
children.description: Rehabilitate stroke, spinal cord injury, cerebral palsy, and Parkinson's patients in hospital neuro units or dedicated rehab centres — emotionally impactful work.
children.roadmap: BPT → MPT Neurology → neuro rehab internship → neurological physiotherapist at corporate or speciality hospital

what_it_is: Restore patient mobility and function through exercise and manual therapy.
who_its_not_for: Those who want desk work or lack physical empathy for patients.
work_lifestyle: Hands-on; patient-facing; hospital, sports club, or own clinic settings.
entry_route: State entrance or direct merit admission; some states use NEET score.
timeline: 4.5 yrs BPT including internship; MPT specialisation adds 2 more yrs.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L employed; ₹10L–₹30L own practice in metro city.
growth_and_progression: Junior physiotherapist → senior → sports or neuro specialist → own clinic.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; sports industry, aging population, and post-COVID rehab driving demand.
honest_caveat: Hospital salaries low; own practice or sports team contracts pay far better.

what_it_is: Pharmacy, physiotherapy, nursing, and paramedical science degree paths.
who_its_not_for: Those targeting MBBS or surgery — these are fully distinct clinical pathways.
work_lifestyle: Hospital, pharmacy, clinic, or rehabilitation centre daily patient work.
entry_route: State pharmacy entrance or direct admission; NEET for some programmes.
timeline: 4-yr B.Pharm or BPT; M.Pharm or MPT adds 2 more years after that.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L depending on role, employer type, and public vs private sector.
growth_and_progression: Clinical specialist, hospital manager, regulatory affairs, or own practice.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's hospitals, pharmacies, and rehab centres constantly hiring.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries lower than engineering; growth requires experience or PG.

children.name: Veterinary, Environmental, and Agricultural Sciences
children.description: Biology-based careers in animal health, ecology, food systems, and environmental management.

children.name: BVSc and AH (Bachelor of Veterinary Science)
children.description: Five-and-a-half year professional veterinary degree accessible via NEET-UG — treating animals and supporting India's livestock and food economy.

children.name: Government Veterinary Officer
children.description: Join state animal husbandry departments as a veterinary officer — treating livestock, running AI programmes, and supporting rural farmers across India.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹14L / yr state veterinary officer scale with all government benefits
children.roadmap: NEET-UG → BVSc 5.5 yrs → state veterinary services exam → veterinary officer → senior VO → district animal husbandry officer

children.name: Companion Animal Specialist or Pet Clinic Owner
children.description: Treat dogs, cats, and exotic pets at urban veterinary clinics — India's fastest-growing veterinary segment driven by rising pet ownership in metros.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹25L / yr (own urban clinic at higher end)
children.roadmap: BVSc → internship at companion animal clinic → associate vet → own pet clinic in metro city → specialist practice

what_it_is: Train to become a licensed veterinarian treating companion and farm animals.
who_its_not_for: Those who are not comfortable with animals, blood, or rural postings.
work_lifestyle: Clinic, farm, and abattoir visits; outdoor fieldwork; varied and hands-on.
entry_route: NEET-UG; separate veterinary quota; roughly 550+ for general category.
timeline: 5.5 yrs BVSc including rotating internship; MVSc adds 2 more years.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L; government veterinary officer roles well-perked and stable.
growth_and_progression: General vet → specialist → government officer → own animal hospital.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India has the world's largest livestock population needing vets.
honest_caveat: Most high-paying roles outside metro cities; urban companion animal niches growing.

children.name: B.Sc Agriculture or Environmental Science
children.description: Four-year agriculture degree or three-year environmental science degree for students drawn to food systems, ecology, and natural resource management.

children.name: Agricultural Development Officer or ICAR Scientist
children.description: Join state agriculture departments or ICAR institutes to support farmers, run crop experiments, and advance India's agricultural science.

children.name: ICAR Agricultural Research Scientist
children.description: Conduct crop improvement, soil health, or agronomy research at ICAR institutes across India — mission-driven government science with full benefits and pension.
children.salaryRange: ₹7L–₹15L / yr ICAR scientist scale with all allowances
children.roadmap: B.Sc Ag → M.Sc → ICAR-ARS NET JRF → PhD at ICAR institute → scientist posting → senior scientist

children.name: State Agricultural Development Officer
children.description: Work in Krishi Vigyan Kendras or district agriculture offices — train farmers, supervise crop demonstrations, and implement government agricultural schemes on the ground.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Ag → M.Sc → state ag services recruitment exam → ADO posting → senior agricultural officer

what_it_is: Government-funded agricultural research and farmer extension work.
who_its_not_for: Those who want city-based careers or commercial sector salaries.
work_lifestyle: Field visits, experiment plots, and farmer training; rural postings.
entry_route: B.Sc Ag → M.Sc Agronomy → ICAR-ARS NET or state ag services exam.
timeline: B.Sc 4 + M.Sc 2 + exam prep 1 yr; posting within 1 yr of qualifying.
salary_range: ₹6L–₹15L / yr government scale with housing and field allowances.
growth_and_progression: Agricultural officer → senior scientist → principal scientist → director.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; ICAR, KVKs, and state ag departments recruit every cycle.
honest_caveat: Rural or semi-rural postings; metro-based roles are very few here.

children.name: Agri-Tech and Agribusiness Manager
children.description: Build or manage technology-driven agricultural ventures — precision farming platforms, cold chain logistics, or input supply companies targeting Indian farmers.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹20L / yr employed; equity upside if founding a startup
children.roadmap: B.Sc Ag → MBA Agribusiness at MANAGE or IRMA → agri-tech company or cooperative → operations or product role → founding or leadership track

children.name: Environmental Consultant or Climate Analyst
children.description: Conduct EIAs, biodiversity surveys, and sustainability reports for industry, government, and international NGOs working in India.

children.name: Environmental Impact Assessment (EIA) Consultant
children.description: Prepare EIA reports for infrastructure, mining, and industrial projects — mandatory under India's EIA Notification 2006 before any project gets MoEF clearance.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Env Sci → M.Sc → intern at EIA consulting firm → junior consultant → EIA project lead in 4–5 yrs

children.name: Corporate Sustainability and ESG Analyst
children.description: Help Indian and multinational companies measure, report, and reduce their environmental footprint — a fast-growing role as SEBI's BRSR mandate expands.
children.roadmap: B.Sc Env Sci → M.Sc or MBA Sustainability → sustainability intern at FMCG or infra company → ESG analyst → sustainability manager

what_it_is: Assessing and managing environmental impacts of development projects.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike fieldwork, report writing, or regulatory complexity.
work_lifestyle: Field surveys and desktop analysis; project deadlines drive the pace.
entry_route: B.Sc Env Sci → M.Sc Environmental Science or Management then internship.
timeline: Consultant in 1–2 yrs post-M.Sc; senior consultant in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹20L consulting firms; international NGOs pay at the higher end.
growth_and_progression: Junior consultant → senior → project lead → partner or own firm.
demand_and_outlook: Growing; India's EIA notification and ESG reporting mandates creating demand.
honest_caveat: Government EIA consulting is crowded; differentiation through specialisation needed.

what_it_is: Crop science, soil science, ecology, and food production degrees.
who_its_not_for: Those who want city-based tech or clinical careers exclusively.
work_lifestyle: Field and lab mix; seasonal cycles; rural and semi-urban postings common.
entry_route: ICAR AIEEA for agricultural universities; CUET for environmental science.
timeline: B.Sc Ag 4 yrs; B.Sc Env 3 yrs; M.Sc or MBA Agri for senior roles.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹18L; government agri officer and MNC agri company roles best paid.
growth_and_progression: Field officer → specialist → government ag officer → agri-entrepreneur.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's food security and climate goals driving long-term demand.
honest_caveat: Mostly rural or semi-rural postings; research needs M.Sc and NET.

what_it_is: Degrees in animal medicine, conservation, agriculture, and environmental science.
who_its_not_for: Those who want urban office careers or patient-facing human medicine.
work_lifestyle: Field, farm, lab, and clinic mix; outdoor and rural work is common.
entry_route: NEET-UG for BVSc; ICAR AIEEA for Ag degrees; CUET for Env Science.
timeline: BVSc 5.5 yrs; B.Sc Ag 4 yrs; B.Sc Env Sci 3 yrs.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L across tracks; government veterinary and ag jobs stable.
growth_and_progression: Specialist vet, government ag officer, conservation scientist, or ag-entrepreneur.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's livestock economy and food security drive consistent hiring.
honest_caveat: Mostly rural or field postings; metro office roles limited in these tracks.

root.name: Commerce
root.description: Career paths for In-School — Commerce in India

children.name: Accounting, Finance, and Professional Certifications
children.description: The gold-standard professional qualification routes for Commerce students in India.

children.name: Chartered Accountancy (CA)
children.description: ICAI's flagship qualification — India's most respected finance credential opening doors to audit, tax, advisory, and CFO roles.

children.name: Audit and Assurance
children.description: Examine and certify the financial statements of companies — the core work of most CA firms and all Big 4 practices in India.

children.name: Statutory Auditor at Big 4 (Deloitte / EY / PwC / KPMG)
children.description: Audit listed companies, banks, and multinationals at India's Big 4 firms — best brand name, highest fresher pay, and strongest exit options in accounting.
children.roadmap: CA Foundation → Intermediate → articleship at Big 4 → Final exam → qualified CA → audit associate → senior associate

children.name: Internal Auditor at Corporates or PSUs
children.description: Audit internal controls, risk management, and compliance at large Indian companies or PSUs — more stable hours and better work-life balance than Big 4.
children.roadmap: CA qualification → internal audit role at corporate or PSU → senior internal auditor → head of internal audit in 8–10 yrs

what_it_is: Verifying company accounts for accuracy and regulatory compliance.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike repetitive documentation or strict rule adherence.
work_lifestyle: Client site audits; tight deadlines; travel to client offices regularly.
entry_route: Qualify CA; join Big 4 or mid-tier CA firm as audit associate.
timeline: Associate → senior associate in 2 yrs; manager in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹7L–₹18L fresher at Big 4; ₹25L–₹60L at manager level.
growth_and_progression: Associate → manager → senior manager → director → partner.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; mandatory statutory audit drives constant demand every year.
honest_caveat: Partner track takes 15–20 yrs; most move to industry before reaching it.

children.name: Taxation and Advisory
children.description: Advise companies and individuals on income tax, GST, international tax, and transfer pricing — the fastest-growing CA practice area in India.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹22L / yr fresher; own practice income highly variable
children.roadmap: CA qualification → tax associate at CA firm or Big 4 tax → senior → manager → own CA practice or Big 4 director

what_it_is: Professional accounting qualification with global recognition and prestige.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to endure years of high-difficulty exams and articleship.
work_lifestyle: Articleship is intense; qualified practice is varied and client-facing.
entry_route: CA Foundation after Class 12; then Intermediate; then Final with articleship.
timeline: Minimum 4.5 yrs; most candidates take 6–8 yrs to clear all levels.
salary_range: ₹7L–₹25L fresher; Big 4 partner earns ₹1Cr+ after 15–20 yrs.
growth_and_progression: Article → qualified CA → manager → partner or CFO track.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every listed company mandates statutory audit by CA.
honest_caveat: Overall pass rate under 10%; articleship stipend as low as ₹2,000/month.

children.name: Cost and Management Accountancy (CMA)
children.description: ICMAI's professional qualification focused on cost management, management accounting, and financial analysis — less competitive than CA with strong industry demand.

children.name: Cost Analyst at Manufacturing or PSU
children.description: Monitor production costs, prepare cost statements, and support pricing decisions at manufacturing companies, PSUs, or FMCG firms across India.
children.roadmap: CMA Foundation → Intermediate → Final with training → cost analyst at manufacturing company → cost manager → CFO track

children.name: Management Accountant at Corporate Finance
children.description: Drive budgeting, forecasting, and business performance analysis at corporate finance teams — a desk-based analytical role with strong career trajectory.
children.roadmap: CMA qualification → management accountant at large corporate → FP&A analyst → senior analyst → finance manager

what_it_is: Professional qualification in cost control, budgeting, and financial management.
who_its_not_for: Those who want statutory audit rights or CA's broader brand recognition.
work_lifestyle: Mostly industry-based roles; cost analysis and management reporting cycles.
entry_route: CMA Foundation after Class 12; Intermediate; Final with practical training.
timeline: 3–4 years to qualify; easier pass rates than CA but respected in industry.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹18L fresher CMA; higher with experience in manufacturing sector.
growth_and_progression: Cost analyst → cost manager → CFO track in manufacturing companies.
demand_and_outlook: Good; manufacturing, PSUs, and BFSI sector value CMA qualification.
honest_caveat: Less recognised than CA for audit and Big 4 roles; best in manufacturing.

children.name: Company Secretary (CS)
children.description: ICSI's professional qualification governing corporate law, secretarial practice, and regulatory compliance — unique niche with no direct substitute.

children.name: Company Secretary at Listed Company
children.description: Handle board meeting logistics, SEBI filings, shareholder communications, and RoC compliance at a listed Indian company — a legally mandated and well-paid role.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹20L / yr (fresher CS to senior CS range)
children.roadmap: CS Foundation → Executive → Professional → training → CS at private company → senior CS at listed company

children.name: Compliance and Governance Consultant
children.description: Work at CS firms or governance advisory practices helping companies with FEMA, SEBI regulations, and corporate restructuring — broader than in-house CS work.
children.salaryRange: ₹6L–₹18L / yr at CS firm; higher in Big 4 governance practice
children.roadmap: CS qualification + LLB → CS firm or Big 4 governance → compliance consultant → senior manager → own CS firm

what_it_is: Qualified professional managing corporate governance and regulatory filings.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike legal text, compliance documentation, or regulatory work.
work_lifestyle: Desk-heavy; board meeting management; SEBI and MCA filing cycles.
entry_route: CS Foundation after Class 12; Executive; Professional with training.
timeline: 3–4 yrs to qualify; most complete while doing BCom or LLB.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹15L fresher CS; ₹20L–₹40L senior company secretary at listed firm.
growth_and_progression: Trainee → CS executive → company secretary → VP compliance or CFO.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; every listed and large private company must appoint a CS.
honest_caveat: Limited seats at top companies; solo practice difficult early in career.

what_it_is: CA, CMA, CS, and finance degrees leading to high-trust financial careers.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike numbers, regulation, or multi-year exam commitments.
work_lifestyle: Desk-heavy; deadline-driven; audit seasons and quarterly close cycles.
entry_route: CA Foundation after Class 12; CMA Foundation; CS Foundation parallel.
timeline: CA takes 4–5 yrs minimum; CMA and CS 3–4 yrs; BCom 3 yrs.
salary_range: ₹7L–₹30L fresher CA; experienced partners earn significantly more.
growth_and_progression: Qualified professional → manager → partner → CFO or own practice.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every company needs auditors, tax advisors, and finance teams.
honest_caveat: CA pass rates are very low; most students take 5–8 yrs to qualify.

children.name: Banking, Financial Services, and Insurance (BFSI)
children.description: Career paths inside banks, NBFCs, insurance companies, and financial markets.

children.name: Banking (Government and Private)
children.description: Work in PSU banks via IBPS or SBI exams, or join private banks like HDFC, ICICI, and Axis through campus or direct recruitment.

children.name: PSU Bank Probationary Officer (IBPS PO or SBI PO)
children.description: Join nationalised banks like Bank of Baroda, PNB, or Canara Bank via IBPS PO exam — stable salary, pension, and structured promotion path.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹9L / yr (PO to officer grade scale with allowances)
children.roadmap: BCom or any graduation → IBPS PO exam → pre and mains → interview → joining → officer → manager in 5 yrs

children.name: Private Bank Relationship Manager or Credit Officer
children.description: Manage retail or business banking relationships, disburse loans, and cross-sell financial products at HDFC, ICICI, Axis, or Kotak — fast-paced and high-earning track.
children.roadmap: BCom or BBA → campus placement at private bank → relationship officer → RM → senior RM → branch manager in 5–7 yrs

what_it_is: Branch banking, credit, operations, and customer relationship management.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike targets, repetitive transactions, or branch work.
work_lifestyle: Branch-based; fixed banking hours; target-driven with quarterly pressure.
entry_route: IBPS PO or SBI PO for government; direct campus for private banks.
timeline: PO joining in 1 yr post-degree; manager in 3–5 yrs in private banks.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹10L government PO; ₹6L–₹15L private bank relationship manager.
growth_and_progression: PO → manager → AGM → DGM → GM → ED at PSU banks.
demand_and_outlook: High; India has 12 PSU banks and 21 private banks all actively hiring.
honest_caveat: PSU bank promotions slow; private bank attrition and pressure very high.

children.name: Investment Banking and Capital Markets
children.description: Work on M&A deals, equity issuances, and debt capital markets at domestic or global investment banks operating in India.

children.name: Investment Banking Analyst (IB)
children.description: Build financial models, prepare pitch books, and execute M&A and equity capital market transactions at domestic or foreign investment banks in Mumbai.
children.roadmap: BCom → top MBA (IIM or ISB) → IB summer internship → analyst offer → associate in 2–3 yrs → VP track

children.name: Equity Research Analyst
children.description: Cover sectors and publish research for institutional investors — a rigorous analytical career at the intersection of finance and business strategy.

children.name: Sell-Side Research Analyst at Brokerage
children.description: Cover a sector and publish research notes for institutional and retail clients at brokerages like Motilal Oswal, Kotak Securities, or ICICI Securities.
children.roadmap: BCom → CFA Level 1 → research intern at brokerage → junior analyst → sector analyst → senior analyst in 4–6 yrs

children.name: Buy-Side Analyst at Mutual Fund or Hedge Fund
children.description: Analyse investment ideas for portfolio managers at AMCs like HDFC Mutual Fund, Mirae, or hedge funds — higher pay and direct P&L accountability than sell-side.
children.roadmap: BCom → CFA → sell-side analyst 2–3 yrs → move buy-side at AMC → portfolio analyst → fund manager track

what_it_is: Analysing companies and sectors to produce buy or sell recommendations.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike deep reading, financial modelling, or writing long reports.
work_lifestyle: Market-hours dependent; earnings season intense; research-driven pace.
entry_route: BCom + CFA Level 1 → internship at brokerage; MBA finance accelerates.
timeline: Junior analyst in 1–2 yrs; sector coverage in 3–4 yrs; senior in 6–8.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹25L sell-side; ₹15L–₹60L buy-side with bonus.
growth_and_progression: Junior analyst → analyst → senior analyst → fund manager track.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; automation threatening basic research; differentiated analysts valued.
honest_caveat: CFA or MBA strongly preferred; Commerce degree alone rarely sufficient.

what_it_is: Advising companies on deals, fundraising, and financial restructuring.
who_its_not_for: Those who cannot handle 80-100 hour work weeks or high-stakes pressure.
work_lifestyle: Long hours; deal-driven sprints; analytical and presentation-heavy work.
entry_route: Top MBA from IIM or ISB; or direct analyst hire from top colleges.
timeline: Analyst → associate in 2–3 yrs; VP in 5–6 yrs; MD in 12–15 yrs.
salary_range: ₹15L–₹40L fresher analyst; ₹50L–₹2Cr at VP and MD level.
growth_and_progression: Analyst → associate → VP → director → managing director.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate at junior level; India M&A market growing steadily.
honest_caveat: Almost impossible without IIM or ISB MBA; direct Commerce entry is rare.

children.name: Insurance and Wealth Management
children.description: Sell and manage insurance policies and investment products for individuals and corporates through banks, insurers, and wealth management firms.

children.name: Wealth Manager at Private Bank or HNI Firm
children.description: Manage HNI and ultra-HNI client portfolios at private banks or wealth firms — India's most lucrative financial advisory career for Commerce graduates.

children.name: Wealth Relationship Manager at Private Bank
children.description: Manage portfolios of HNI clients (₹50L–₹5Cr investable assets) at HDFC Private, Kotak, or Axis Wealth — cross-selling mutual funds, insurance, and structured products.
children.roadmap: BCom or BBA → NISM Series V-A → junior wealth advisor → RM at private bank → senior RM → cluster head

children.name: Independent Financial Advisor (IFA) or RIA
children.description: Register as a SEBI-registered investment advisor and build an independent fee-based practice advising retail and HNI clients on financial planning and portfolio construction.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹50L+ / yr (entirely AUM and fee-dependent; highly variable)
children.roadmap: BCom → CFP certification → AMFI ARN → junior at wealth firm → 3–5 yrs experience → SEBI RIA registration → independent practice

what_it_is: Managing investments and financial planning for wealthy individuals.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike deep client relationships or AUM-based performance pressure.
work_lifestyle: Client meetings; portfolio reviews; relationship-driven; flexible hours.
entry_route: BCom or BBA → NISM or CFP certification → junior wealth advisor role.
timeline: Advisor in 1–2 yrs; senior RM in 4–5 yrs; private banking in 6–8.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹30L base; AUM-linked bonus can multiply earnings significantly.
growth_and_progression: Junior advisor → relationship manager → senior RM → private banker.
demand_and_outlook: High; India's HNI population growing 11% per year; demand far exceeds supply.
honest_caveat: Book building takes 5–7 yrs; early income dependent on base only.

children.name: Insurance Underwriter or Product Manager
children.description: Assess risk, price policies, and design insurance products at life or general insurance companies like LIC, HDFC Life, or New India Assurance.
children.roadmap: BCom or BBA → direct hire at insurer → junior underwriter → senior underwriter → product manager or actuary track

what_it_is: Advising clients on protection, savings, and investment products.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike sales targets or cold client acquisition work.
work_lifestyle: Client meetings; target-driven; flexible but performance-measured hours.
entry_route: BCom or BBA direct hire; IRDA exam for insurance agents and advisors.
timeline: Direct hire fresher; senior advisor in 3–5 yrs with strong performance.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹20L base; commissions and incentives can double total earnings.
growth_and_progression: Advisor → senior advisor → relationship manager → team leader → branch head.
demand_and_outlook: High; India's insurance penetration at 4% versus global average of 7%.
honest_caveat: Income highly variable early on; clients hard to acquire without network.

what_it_is: Working within India's massive and growing financial services industry.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike targets, regulatory pressure, or client-facing work.
work_lifestyle: Branch, office, or market-hours environment; target-driven culture.
entry_route: BCom or BBA then bank PO exam or direct placement; MBA for fast track.
timeline: PO in 1–2 yrs post-graduation; private bank hire immediately after degree.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L in government banks; ₹6L–₹30L in private and investment banks.
growth_and_progression: PO → manager → senior manager → AGM → DGM → GM at PSU banks.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; India's financial inclusion push and BFSI growth driving hiring.
honest_caveat: Government bank culture is bureaucratic; private bank attrition is high.

children.name: Management, Strategy, and Entrepreneurship
children.description: Business and management degree paths leading to corporate strategy, consulting, and founding roles.

children.name: BBA or BCom (General Management)
children.description: Three-year undergraduate management or commerce degree — the foundation for most corporate, banking, and entrepreneurship careers from a Commerce background.

children.name: Marketing and Brand Management
children.description: Work in marketing teams at FMCG, e-commerce, or consumer brands — one of the most accessible and creative Commerce career paths in India.

children.name: Digital Marketing Executive
children.description: Run paid ads, SEO campaigns, and social media for brands or marketing agencies — the entry point for most Commerce graduates into the marketing function.
children.roadmap: BCom or BBA → Google or Meta digital marketing cert → marketing intern → digital marketing executive → senior executive → digital marketing manager

children.name: Brand Manager at FMCG or Consumer Company
children.description: Own a product's positioning, packaging, advertising, and sales strategy at companies like HUL, ITC, Dabur, or Marico — the most prestigious marketing role in India.
children.roadmap: BCom → IIM or top MBA → FMCG summer internship → brand management trainee → assistant brand manager → brand manager

what_it_is: Building consumer awareness, preference, and loyalty for brands.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike creativity, ambiguity, or consumer psychology.
work_lifestyle: Campaign planning; agency coordination; data-driven iteration cycles.
entry_route: BBA or BCom → digital marketing certification → marketing executive role.
timeline: Executive on graduation; brand manager in 4–5 yrs; director in 8–10.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹12L fresher; ₹20L–₹50L brand director with MBA background.
growth_and_progression: Marketing executive → brand manager → marketing head → CMO.
demand_and_outlook: High; every consumer brand actively hiring digital and brand talent.
honest_caveat: Digital skills mandatory now; traditional BCom alone is insufficient.

children.name: Sales and Business Development
children.description: Drive revenue by acquiring clients and managing accounts — the highest-volume hiring role for Commerce graduates across all sectors in India.

children.name: B2C Sales Executive (FMCG or Fintech)
children.description: Sell consumer goods, insurance, or fintech products to retail customers and retailers — high volume, fast feedback, and the fastest way to build commercial acumen.
children.roadmap: BBA or BCom → campus placement at FMCG or fintech → sales trainee → sales executive → ASM → area sales manager

children.name: B2B Business Development Manager (SaaS or IT)
children.description: Acquire and grow enterprise clients for SaaS, IT services, or B2B platform companies — higher ticket size, longer sales cycles, and significantly better pay than B2C.
children.roadmap: BBA or BCom → BDR or SDR role at SaaS company → account executive → senior AE → BDM → sales manager

what_it_is: Selling products or services and building long-term business relationships.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike targets, rejection, or external-facing daily interactions.
work_lifestyle: Field or office; target cycles; quarterly pressure; high energy required.
entry_route: BBA or BCom direct campus hire; no exam needed; strong communication vital.
timeline: Sales executive on graduation; BDM in 2–3 yrs; manager in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L base; variable component can double or triple for top performers.
growth_and_progression: Sales executive → BDM → regional manager → VP sales → CEO.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every company always hiring sales talent at every level.
honest_caveat: Income inconsistent early on; pressure and attrition high in this function.

what_it_is: Broad business education covering marketing, finance, HR, and operations.
who_its_not_for: Those who need a professional licence or technical depth from day one.
work_lifestyle: Campus placements; entry-level corporate roles; fast learning curve.
entry_route: CUET for Delhi University BCom (H); state entrance for BBA; IPU CET.
timeline: 3 yrs BBA or BCom; then MBA or direct employment or CA pursuit.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹8L fresher BBA or BCom; much higher after MBA or CA.
growth_and_progression: Executive → executive → manager → senior manager over 5–8 yrs.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; good colleges and strong CAs dominate over generic BBA holders.
honest_caveat: Degree alone insufficient at most companies; skills and certifications essential.

children.name: MBA (Post-Graduation)
children.description: Two-year postgraduate management degree — the single biggest career accelerator for Commerce graduates aiming for strategy, consulting, or finance leadership.

children.name: Management Consulting
children.description: Solve strategic and operational problems for India's top corporates at McKinsey, BCG, Bain, or Big 4 consulting firms after an elite MBA.

children.name: MBB Strategy Consultant (McKinsey / BCG / Bain)
children.description: Work on high-stakes strategic projects for Indian and global clients — cost transformation, market entry, M&A integration — at India's most prestigious consulting firms.
children.roadmap: BCom → IIM A or B or C → case prep and consulting club → summer internship → PPO or full-time offer → associate → manager

children.name: Big 4 Strategy and Operations Consultant (Deloitte / EY / PwC / KPMG)
children.description: Deliver operational improvement, digital transformation, and post-merger integration projects for large Indian companies and government clients at Big 4 advisory.
children.roadmap: BCom → IIM or top MBA → Big 4 advisory → analyst → senior analyst → manager → senior manager

what_it_is: Advising CXOs on strategy, transformation, and operational improvement.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike ambiguity, frequent travel, or relentless client pressure.
work_lifestyle: Monday-to-Friday travel; project-based; structured but high-intensity.
entry_route: IIM A, B, C or ISB MBA; consulting clubs and case prep essential.
timeline: Analyst or associate on joining; manager in 3 yrs; partner in 8–10.
salary_range: ₹25L–₹50L fresher MBA at MBB; ₹18L–₹30L at Big 4 strategy.
growth_and_progression: Associate → manager → senior manager → partner track.
demand_and_outlook: High; MBB and Big 4 expand India offices every year.
honest_caveat: MBB hiring from IIMs is under 50 seats per campus; brutally selective.

children.name: Finance and Investment Management (MBA)
children.description: Join BFSI firms, PE funds, or corporate finance teams after an MBA in finance — the most lucrative MBA specialisation for Commerce background students.
children.roadmap: BCom + CFA → IIM or ISB MBA Finance → IB or PE summer intern → analyst or associate at BFSI firm → VP track

what_it_is: Intensive management education with peer network and placement access.
who_its_not_for: Those not ready for 2 yrs of intense coursework and recruitment grind.
work_lifestyle: Case studies; group projects; exhausting but high-energy campus life.
entry_route: CAT for IIMs; XAT for XLRI; GMAT for ISB; 2–4 yrs work experience ideal.
timeline: 2 yr MBA; top IIM campus placements within 2 yrs of graduation.
salary_range: ₹15L–₹35L fresher from IIM A, B, C; ₹8L–₹15L from Tier-2 IIMs.
growth_and_progression: Post-MBA analyst → manager → senior manager → director → CXO.
demand_and_outlook: High from consulting, BFSI, FMCG, and tech firms for top MBA grads.
honest_caveat: Only IIM A, B, C, L, K, I, XLRI, ISB deliver transformational returns.

children.name: Entrepreneurship and Startups
children.description: Build a company from scratch leveraging Commerce skills in fintech, e-commerce, consumer brands, or B2B services.

children.name: Fintech or E-Commerce Founder
children.description: Build a consumer or B2B fintech, direct-to-consumer brand, or marketplace leveraging Commerce knowledge of financial systems and consumer behaviour.
children.salaryRange: ₹0–₹15L founder salary; equity upside if funded and scaled
children.roadmap: BCom or MBA → 2–3 yrs at startup or bank → identify problem → co-founder + MVP → pre-seed or seed funding → scale

children.name: Small Business and Family Business Professional
children.description: Transform or professionalise a family business using modern management, digital marketing, and financial discipline — increasingly high-value in India's SME economy.
children.salaryRange: Business profit varies widely; ₹5L–₹50L+ personal income depending on scale
children.roadmap: BCom or BBA → MBA or CA → join family business → introduce systems and processes → expand or diversify → professionalised leadership

what_it_is: Starting or joining early-stage companies as founder or core team member.
who_its_not_for: Those who need salary certainty or cannot tolerate prolonged uncertainty.
work_lifestyle: All-hands; no boundaries; founder wears every hat simultaneously.
entry_route: 2–3 yrs industry experience; build network; identify problem; raise funding.
timeline: 2–4 yrs industry then founding; 3–5 yrs to product-market fit typically.
salary_range: ₹0–₹10L founder salary initially; wealth via equity if venture succeeds.
growth_and_progression: Idea → MVP → seed funding → Series A → scale or acquisition.
demand_and_outlook: India's startup ecosystem active; 100+ unicorns and growing.
honest_caveat: 90%+ startups fail; founders need savings runway of 18–24 months minimum.

what_it_is: BBA, BCom, and MBA paths into management, consulting, and business.
who_its_not_for: Those who prefer deep technical or regulatory specialist careers.
work_lifestyle: Dynamic; client or cross-functional; presentations and stakeholder management.
entry_route: BBA or BCom direct admission; CUET for central university BCom; MBA later.
timeline: 3-yr BBA or BCom; 2-yr MBA post work experience for fast-track roles.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹15L after BBA or BCom; ₹15L–₹50L post IIM or ISB MBA.
growth_and_progression: Analyst → manager → senior manager → director → CXO or founder.
demand_and_outlook: High across consulting, FMCG, e-commerce, and startup sectors.
honest_caveat: MBA ROI depends entirely on institute tier; Tier-3 MBA adds little value.

children.name: Law and Regulatory Careers
children.description: Legal and compliance careers uniquely accessible and relevant for Commerce background students.

children.name: Corporate and Tax Law (LLB or BCom LLB)
children.description: Practice corporate law, M&A transactions, tax disputes, and regulatory compliance at law firms or as in-house counsel at companies.

children.name: Corporate M&A and Transactions Lawyer
children.description: Draft and negotiate transaction documents for mergers, acquisitions, and private equity deals at top-tier Indian law firms like AZB, Cyril Amarchand, or Shardul.
children.roadmap: CLAT → NLU → BCom LLB → intern at top law firm → junior associate → M&A transactions team → senior associate → partner track

children.name: In-House Legal Counsel at Corporate
children.description: Work as the internal legal team for a large Indian or multinational company — managing contracts, litigation, compliance, and regulatory matters without the law firm grind.
children.roadmap: BCom LLB or NLU → 2–3 yrs at law firm → in-house legal role at MNC or large Indian company → senior counsel → general counsel

children.name: Tax Litigation and Advisory Lawyer
children.description: Handle income tax, GST, and customs disputes before tribunals and high courts — one of the most lucrative specialisations for Commerce-background lawyers in India.
children.salaryRange: ₹5L–₹15L junior; ₹30L–₹1Cr+ senior partner or senior advocate
children.roadmap: BCom + LLB or BCom LLB → apprentice at tax chambers → junior advocate → independent tax practice or law firm partner in 8–12 yrs

what_it_is: Legal advisory for businesses on contracts, deals, and regulatory matters.
who_its_not_for: Those who want courtroom litigation or criminal law careers.
work_lifestyle: Law firm or in-house office; long hours during transactions; document-heavy.
entry_route: CLAT for NLUs; or BCom then 3-yr LLB; BCom LLB integrated is ideal.
timeline: 5 yrs BCom LLB or 3 yrs LLB; junior at law firm; 7–10 yrs to seniority.
salary_range: ₹4L–₹12L junior; ₹20L–₹80L senior at top-tier law firms.
growth_and_progression: Junior → associate → senior associate → partner at law firm.
demand_and_outlook: High; M&A boom, SEBI regulations, and GST litigation driving demand.
honest_caveat: Top NLU degree almost mandatory for top law firm partnership track.

children.name: Regulatory and Compliance Careers
children.description: Work in regulatory bodies like SEBI, RBI, or IRDAI, or in compliance functions at BFSI firms — a stable high-responsibility career requiring Commerce and legal knowledge.

children.name: SEBI or RBI Officer (Grade A)
children.description: Join SEBI as a market regulator or RBI as a central bank officer — prestigious, secure, and intellectually engaging government careers for Commerce graduates.
children.salaryRange: ₹12L–₹20L / yr Grade A CTC with allowances
children.roadmap: BCom or BA Economics → SEBI Grade A or RBI Grade B exam prep → Phase 1 and 2 → interview → joining → Grade B in 4–5 yrs

children.name: Compliance Officer at BFSI Company
children.description: Manage regulatory compliance, AML programmes, and SEBI or RBI reporting requirements at a bank, NBFC, or insurance company — a desk-based in-demand role.
children.roadmap: BCom + CS or CA + LLB → compliance analyst at BFSI firm → senior compliance officer → head of compliance in 8–10 yrs

what_it_is: Enforcing or managing financial regulations for stability and investor protection.
who_its_not_for: Those who want fast promotions or high commercial income early.
work_lifestyle: Desk-heavy; regulatory research; filing and review cycles; government pace.
entry_route: SEBI Grade A or RBI Grade B exam; or compliance role via CA or CS.
timeline: Exam prep 1–2 yrs post-graduation; Grade A joining; promotion every 3–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹12L–₹20L SEBI Grade A; ₹15L–₹35L private sector compliance head.
growth_and_progression: Grade A → B → C → Executive Director at regulator; or move to industry.
demand_and_outlook: Steady; India's regulatory framework expanding with new laws every year.
honest_caveat: Government regulator lifestyle is slow-paced; private compliance pays better.

what_it_is: LLB and LLM paths into corporate law, tax law, and financial regulation.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike extensive reading, argumentation, or procedural work.
work_lifestyle: Court, chamber, or office work; deadline-driven; research and drafting heavy.
entry_route: 5-yr BA LLB or BCom LLB; or 3-yr LLB after any graduation; CLAT exam.
timeline: 5-yr integrated or 3-yr LLB; 2–3 yrs junior practice before independence.
salary_range: ₹3L–₹12L junior; ₹20L–₹1Cr senior corporate lawyer after 10–15 yrs.
growth_and_progression: Junior advocate → associate → senior associate → partner or in-house counsel.
demand_and_outlook: High in corporate and tax law; India's regulatory complexity creating demand.
honest_caveat: Junior advocate income very low; 5–8 years before meaningful earnings.

root.name: Computer Science
root.description: Career paths for B.Tech / B.E. — Computer Science in India

children.name: Software Engineering and Product Development
children.description: Building and shipping software products at India's tech companies — the largest employment category for CS graduates.

children.name: Product Company Engineering
children.description: Work at tech-first companies where software is the product — FAANG-tier, unicorns, and high-growth startups paying the highest engineering salaries in India.

children.name: Backend Engineering
children.description: Build the server-side systems, APIs, and databases that power products — the most common SDE specialisation at product companies.

children.name: Backend SDE at FAANG or Tier-1 Product Company
children.description: Build high-scale APIs, microservices, and distributed systems at Google, Microsoft, Flipkart, Swiggy, or similar — highest compensation in Indian tech.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → CP from Year 1 → FAANG-style internship → SDE-1 offer → SDE-2 in 2–3 yrs → senior SDE

children.name: Backend SDE at Growth-Stage Startup
children.description: Own entire backend services end-to-end at a Series B or C startup — lower pay than FAANG but much higher ownership, speed, and potential equity upside.
children.salaryRange: ₹12L–₹30L / yr (plus equity that may compound significantly)
children.roadmap: B.Tech → startup internship → SDE-1 at startup → service owner in 1–2 yrs → senior SDE or tech lead

what_it_is: Engineering the logic, data, and services behind every user-facing product.
who_its_not_for: Those who want visual or user-facing work exclusively.
work_lifestyle: Deep technical work; cross-team APIs; on-call rotations at senior levels.
entry_route: Strong DSA; system design; Java, Go, or Python proficiency demonstrated.
timeline: Backend SDE-1 on graduation; owns services independently in 2–3 yrs.
salary_range: ₹20L–₹55L fresher at top product companies; lower at mid-tier.
growth_and_progression: SDE-1 → SDE-2 → senior → tech lead → staff engineer.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every product company's core engineering is backend-heavy.
honest_caveat: On-call pressure and production incidents are real at senior levels.

children.name: Frontend and Full-Stack Engineering
children.description: Build user interfaces and end-to-end features spanning client and server — high demand at startups and product companies building consumer applications.

children.name: Frontend Engineer at Product Company
children.description: Build fast, accessible, and scalable user interfaces at consumer tech companies like Zomato, PhonePe, or Razorpay — closest to the end user in the engineering org.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → React and TypeScript projects → frontend intern → SDE-1 frontend → senior frontend engineer in 3–4 yrs

children.name: Full-Stack Engineer at Startup
children.description: Own entire product features end-to-end — from database schema to UI — at a seed or Series A startup where every engineer ships across the full stack.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → full-stack projects on GitHub → startup internship → SDE-1 → feature owner in 1–2 yrs → senior full-stack engineer

what_it_is: Engineering interfaces and full product flows from browser to database.
who_its_not_for: Those who want deep backend infrastructure or systems-level work only.
work_lifestyle: Close to product and design; rapid iteration; user feedback loops fast.
entry_route: React and TypeScript projects; full-stack internship; open-source contribution.
timeline: Full-stack SDE-1 on graduation; senior in 3–4 yrs; lead in 5–6 yrs.
salary_range: ₹12L–₹45L depending on company tier and stack depth.
growth_and_progression: SDE-1 → senior → tech lead → frontend architect or engineering manager.
demand_and_outlook: High; every consumer product company needs strong frontend talent.
honest_caveat: Frontend frameworks change fast; continuous learning is non-negotiable.

what_it_is: Building consumer or enterprise products at tech-first organisations.
who_its_not_for: Those who need immediate stability without months of interview preparation.
work_lifestyle: Fast iteration cycles; high ownership; cross-functional collaboration daily.
entry_route: Competitive programming; internships; FAANG-style OA and interviews.
timeline: SDE-1 on day one; senior SDE in 3–4 yrs; staff in 6–8 yrs.
salary_range: ₹20L–₹60L+ fresher CTC including stock at top-tier companies.
growth_and_progression: SDE-1 → SDE-2 → senior → staff → principal or EM track.
demand_and_outlook: Extremely high; shortage of interview-ready candidates at every level.
honest_caveat: Top pay concentrated at under 50 companies; interview process brutal.

children.name: IT Services and Consulting Engineering
children.description: Work on client delivery projects at large IT services firms — the most accessible engineering path with the widest campus reach across India.

children.name: Application Developer at IT Services
children.description: Build and maintain enterprise applications for BFSI, retail, and manufacturing clients at large Indian IT services companies — the most accessible CS career path.

children.name: Java Developer at BFSI IT Services
children.description: Build and maintain core banking, insurance, and payment applications for global BFSI clients at TCS, Infosys BFS, or Wipro BFSI — stable, structured, but slow-growing.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → on-campus at TCS or Infosys → Java training → BFSI project → upskill in system design → lateral move to product company

children.name: Python or Automation Developer at IT Services
children.description: Build automation scripts, data pipelines, and test frameworks for enterprise clients — a more modern stack than legacy Java and better positioned for lateral moves.
children.salaryRange: ₹4L–₹10L / yr fresher; ₹12L–₹22L after upskilling and lateral move
children.roadmap: B.Tech → automation or Python project → IT services placement → automation engineer → upskill in cloud or ML → lateral to product company

what_it_is: Delivering client software projects in structured, process-heavy environments.
who_its_not_for: Those who want product ownership, high pay, or fast technical growth.
work_lifestyle: Client requirement driven; structured delivery; potential shift work.
entry_route: On-campus placement at TCS, Infosys, Wipro, Cognizant, or HCL.
timeline: Fresher → associate in 2 yrs; senior in 4–5 yrs; lateral move advised.
salary_range: ₹3.5L–₹8L fresher; ₹10L–₹18L after 3–4 yrs upskilling.
growth_and_progression: Associate → senior → lead → manager; or lateral exit to product.
demand_and_outlook: Very high volume; tens of thousands of seats opened every year.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries and growth significantly lower than product companies.

children.name: Cloud and DevOps Engineer at IT Services
children.description: Migrate, manage, and automate client cloud infrastructure at IT services firms — one of the better-paid and more technical roles within IT services.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → cloud certification (AWS SAA) → IT services cloud project → DevOps engineer → senior DevOps → move to product company or cloud firm

what_it_is: Building and maintaining software for enterprise clients globally.
who_its_not_for: Those who want high pay, product ownership, or fast technical growth.
work_lifestyle: Structured; client-requirement driven; process-heavy; shift work possible.
entry_route: On-campus placement at TCS, Infosys, Wipro, HCL, Cognizant, or Tech Mahindra.
timeline: Fresher → associate in 2 yrs; senior in 4–5 yrs; lateral move common.
salary_range: ₹3.5L–₹8L fresher; rises faster through lateral moves to product firms.
growth_and_progression: Associate → senior → lead → manager; or lateral exit to product companies.
demand_and_outlook: Very high volume; tens of thousands of seats across IT services every year.
honest_caveat: Starting salaries and growth significantly lower than product companies.

what_it_is: Writing, testing, and deploying software used by real users at scale.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike debugging, abstraction, or screen-heavy iterative work.
work_lifestyle: Agile sprints; code reviews; stand-ups; largely remote-friendly across India.
entry_route: Strong DSA and system design; campus placement or off-campus drives.
timeline: SDE-1 on graduation; SDE-2 in 2–3 yrs; senior in 4–5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹60L+ fresher depending entirely on company tier.
growth_and_progression: Staff engineer, engineering manager, or product manager track.
demand_and_outlook: Consistently the highest-demand category for CS graduates in India.
honest_caveat: College tier shapes first offer; Leetcode preparation from Year 1 is essential.

children.name: Data, AI, and Machine Learning
children.description: Extracting insight and building intelligent systems from data — the fastest-growing specialisation for CS graduates in India.

children.name: Machine Learning Engineering
children.description: Build, deploy, and maintain production ML systems — pipelines, feature stores, and model serving infrastructure at scale.

children.name: ML Engineer at Product Company
children.description: Build feature pipelines, model serving layers, and monitoring infrastructure for ML products at tech or BFSI companies.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → ML electives and Kaggle → ML intern → MLE-1 → senior MLE in 3–4 yrs → ML architect

children.name: AI Research Engineer
children.description: Work at the frontier of model research at labs like Google DeepMind India, Microsoft Research, or AI startups — publishing papers while building novel systems.
children.roadmap: B.Tech with strong math → research internship → M.Tech thesis or MS abroad → research engineer at AI lab → research scientist

what_it_is: Engineering the infrastructure that takes ML models to production reliably.
who_its_not_for: Those who want only research without production deployment responsibilities.
work_lifestyle: Heavy engineering focus; on-call for model drift; cross-functional work.
entry_route: ML electives; PyTorch and MLflow projects; ML internship in B.Tech.
timeline: MLE on graduation; senior MLE in 3–4 yrs; ML architect in 6–8 yrs.
salary_range: ₹14L–₹50L at product companies; lower at IT services BFSI teams.
growth_and_progression: MLE → senior MLE → staff MLE → ML platform lead or AI PM.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every company building AI products actively needs MLE talent.
honest_caveat: Role titles vary widely; many MLE roles are closer to data engineering.

children.name: Data Science and Analytics
children.description: Analyse business data and build predictive models to drive decisions at tech, BFSI, e-commerce, and consumer companies.

children.name: Data Scientist at Tech or BFSI
children.description: Build predictive models for product, risk, or operations teams — credit scoring, churn prediction, or recommendation systems at Indian tech and finance companies.

children.name: Data Scientist at E-Commerce or Consumer Tech
children.description: Build recommendation engines, search ranking, and pricing models at companies like Flipkart, Meesho, or Amazon India — directly impacting millions of daily users.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → ML and SQL projects → DS intern at e-commerce → junior DS → senior DS in 3–4 yrs → DS lead

children.name: Data Scientist at BFSI (Risk or Fraud)
children.description: Build credit scoring, fraud detection, and risk models at banks, NBFCs, or fintech companies — high-stakes models that directly affect lending decisions.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → BFSI DS internship → risk or fraud analyst → credit or fraud model owner → lead DS or senior DS in 4 yrs

what_it_is: Using data and models to drive measurable business decisions and outcomes.
who_its_not_for: Those who dislike stakeholder communication or ambiguous problem definitions.
work_lifestyle: Notebook and pipeline work; stakeholder presentations; A/B testing cycles.
entry_route: ML electives; SQL and Python projects; Kaggle competitions; DS internship.
timeline: Junior DS or analyst on graduation; senior DS in 3–4 yrs; lead in 6 yrs.
salary_range: ₹10L–₹35L depending on company tier and domain applied to.
growth_and_progression: DS → senior DS → DS lead → head of analytics or pivot to MLE.
demand_and_outlook: High across BFSI, e-commerce, healthtech, and edtech sectors in India.
honest_caveat: Strong stats and communication both required; only coding is insufficient.

children.name: Data Engineer
children.description: Build and maintain data pipelines, warehouses, and lakehouse architectures that feed analytics and ML systems — the infrastructure layer of every data-driven company.

children.name: Data Engineer at Product Company
children.description: Build and own data pipelines, feature stores, and streaming infrastructure at tech companies like Flipkart, Paytm, or CRED — high scale and high ownership.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → SQL and Python projects → DE intern → data engineer → senior DE → data architect in 5–7 yrs

children.name: Analytics Engineer at Startup or BFSI
children.description: Build clean data models, metrics layers, and BI pipelines at growth-stage startups or BFSI companies — closer to analytics than platform engineering.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → SQL and dbt projects → analytics engineer role → senior AE → data team lead or pivot to DS

what_it_is: Engineering reliable data movement, storage, and transformation at scale.
who_its_not_for: Those who want modelling and insight work rather than infrastructure.
work_lifestyle: Pipeline debugging; schema design; cross-team data contracts; on-call.
entry_route: SQL and Python proficiency; data engineering internship; dbt and Airflow projects.
timeline: DE on graduation; senior DE in 3–4 yrs; data architect in 6–8 yrs.
salary_range: ₹10L–₹40L depending on company size and data platform maturity.
growth_and_progression: DE → senior DE → lead DE → data architect → head of data platform.
demand_and_outlook: High; every company with real analytics investment needs data engineers.
honest_caveat: Often unglamorous work; pipeline failures and data quality issues are daily.

what_it_is: Using data and statistical models to answer business questions and forecast.
who_its_not_for: Those who want pure software engineering without stakeholder communication.
work_lifestyle: Notebook and pipeline work; stakeholder presentations; iterative analysis.
entry_route: ML electives; SQL and Python proficiency; DS internship; Kaggle competitions.
timeline: DS analyst or junior DS on graduation; senior DS in 3–4 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹35L depending on company and domain applied to.
growth_and_progression: DS → senior DS → lead DS → head of analytics or MLE track.
demand_and_outlook: High; demand across BFSI, e-commerce, health-tech, and edtech sectors.
honest_caveat: Strong maths and communication both required; only coding is insufficient.

what_it_is: Data engineering, ML modelling, and AI product development at scale.
who_its_not_for: Those uncomfortable with maths, statistics, or ambiguous open-ended problems.
work_lifestyle: Mix of research, coding, and stakeholder communication at iterative pace.
entry_route: ML electives and Kaggle competitions in B.Tech; targeted ML internships.
timeline: MLE or DS role on graduation; senior in 3–4 yrs; lead in 6–8 yrs.
salary_range: ₹12L–₹50L depending on company tier and specialisation depth.
growth_and_progression: Senior MLE or DS → ML architect → AI product manager or research lead.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every company building AI products needs ML talent urgently.
honest_caveat: Many AI roles are basic data wrangling in practice; verify before joining.

children.name: Cybersecurity and Infrastructure
children.description: Protecting systems, networks, and data — a fast-growing niche with a persistent skill shortage across India's enterprise and BFSI sectors.

children.name: Application and Network Security
children.description: Test and secure web applications, APIs, and network infrastructure against attack — pen testing and red teaming are the most visible roles in this track.

children.name: Penetration Tester (Red Team)
children.description: Legally attack systems, networks, and applications to find vulnerabilities before malicious actors — at security consulting firms or enterprise red teams.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → CTF and bug bounty → OSCP cert → security intern → pen tester → senior red team engineer in 3–4 yrs

children.name: Security Operations Centre (SOC) Analyst
children.description: Monitor, detect, and respond to live cyber threats at SOCs serving Indian banks, enterprises, or MSSPs — high volume, high demand, and clear skill ladder.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → networking and Linux fundamentals → SOC tier-1 analyst → tier-2 → SOC lead or security engineer in 3–4 yrs

what_it_is: Finding vulnerabilities before attackers do through ethical hacking and testing.
who_its_not_for: Those who want predictable 9-to-5 work without ambiguity or deep dives.
work_lifestyle: Project-based engagements; CTF mindset; occasional travel to client sites.
entry_route: CTF competitions; bug bounty programmes; OSCP or CEH certification.
timeline: Junior pen tester in 1 yr; independent tester in 3 yrs; lead in 5 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹35L; HackerOne and bug bounty income supplements fixed salary.
growth_and_progression: Pen tester → senior → red team lead → CISO advisory track.
demand_and_outlook: High; RBI and SEBI mandate periodic pen testing of all regulated entities.
honest_caveat: Bug bounty income highly variable; base salary at consulting firms modest.

children.name: Cloud and Platform Engineering
children.description: Build and operate the cloud infrastructure, developer platforms, and SRE systems that keep modern software running at scale.

children.name: Site Reliability Engineer (SRE)
children.description: Maintain uptime, reduce toil, and improve reliability of large-scale production systems at tech companies — a senior engineering role with significant compensation.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → cloud and Linux fundamentals → DevOps or infra intern → SRE role → senior SRE → platform lead in 4–5 yrs

children.name: Cloud Solutions Architect
children.description: Design scalable, secure, and cost-optimised cloud architectures on AWS, Azure, or GCP for enterprises and product companies across India.

children.name: AWS Solutions Architect at Product Company
children.description: Own cloud architecture decisions for an engineering organisation — designing for scale, cost, and security on AWS at high-growth Indian or global tech companies.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → AWS SAA → cloud engineer → senior cloud engineer → solutions architect in 5–6 yrs → principal architect

children.name: Cloud Consultant at IT Services or Big 4
children.description: Help enterprise clients design and migrate to cloud at Infosys, TCS Pace, Accenture, or Deloitte — client-facing role combining cloud expertise with advisory skills.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → cloud cert → IT services cloud project → senior cloud consultant → architect at consulting firm → principal consultant

what_it_is: Designing the cloud blueprint that entire engineering teams build upon.
who_its_not_for: Those who want hands-on coding over design, governance, and consultation.
work_lifestyle: Client or internal consultations; architecture reviews; cost optimisation.
entry_route: AWS SAA then professional cert; 3–5 yrs cloud engineering experience needed.
timeline: Cloud engineer 3 yrs → solutions architect; principal architect in 7–10 yrs.
salary_range: ₹20L–₹60L / yr; AWS and GCP professional architects well-compensated.
growth_and_progression: Solutions architect → senior → principal → distinguished or VP cloud.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every enterprise cloud migration needs architecture guidance.
honest_caveat: Needs broad experience; junior cloud engineers should not rush this title.

what_it_is: Engineering the reliable infrastructure layer underneath every product.
who_its_not_for: Those who want product feature work or direct user-facing impact.
work_lifestyle: On-call rotations; incident management; automation-first mindset required.
entry_route: Cloud certification (AWS SAA); Kubernetes projects; SRE internship helpful.
timeline: Cloud engineer on graduation; senior in 3 yrs; principal in 5–7 yrs.
salary_range: ₹10L–₹45L; cloud and SRE roles at product companies well-compensated.
growth_and_progression: Cloud engineer → senior → platform lead → VP infrastructure.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; India's cloud migration wave creating years of demand ahead.
honest_caveat: On-call culture can be intense; incident fatigue is a real career risk.

what_it_is: Defending and auditing digital infrastructure against real-world threats.
who_its_not_for: Those who want quick results or dislike deep technical rabbit-holing.
work_lifestyle: Proactive testing; incident response; mostly desk with some travel.
entry_route: B.Tech CSE; security electives; CEH or OSCP certification; CTF participation.
timeline: Security analyst on graduation; engineer in 2–3 yrs; architect in 5–7 yrs.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹40L; specialist skills command strong premium over generalist SDE roles.
growth_and_progression: Security analyst → engineer → architect → CISO or independent consultant.
demand_and_outlook: Very high; every bank and enterprise needs dedicated security talent now.
honest_caveat: Skills must be continuously updated; certification treadmill is very real.

children.name: Research, Higher Education, and Non-Industry Paths
children.description: Academic research, advanced degrees, and government science careers for CS graduates who prioritise knowledge creation over industry roles.

children.name: M.Tech at IIT or NIT via GATE
children.description: Two-year postgraduate degree in CS specialisations — the Indian route to research roles, IIT faculty preparation, and senior technical positions.

children.name: IIT M.Tech in AI or Systems
children.description: Pursue M.Tech in AI, machine learning, or computer systems at IIT Bombay, Delhi, Madras, or Kanpur — opens research scientist and senior MLE roles.
children.salaryRange: ₹20L–₹50L / yr post-M.Tech placement at premium companies
children.roadmap: B.Tech → GATE prep (6–12 months) → score 700+ → IIT M.Tech admission → thesis → campus placement or PhD

children.name: NIT M.Tech and Industry Placement
children.description: Complete M.Tech at a top NIT and leverage the improved brand for senior SDE or analyst roles — a practical upgrade for mid-tier B.Tech graduates.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → GATE score 600+ → NIT M.Tech → thesis project → campus placement → senior SDE or specialist role

what_it_is: Advanced technical study in AI, systems, security, or theory at premier institutes.
who_its_not_for: Those who want industry income within a year of B.Tech completion.
work_lifestyle: Coursework, thesis research, lab work; stipend-supported lifestyle.
entry_route: GATE CS; score above 700 for IIT M.Tech; 600+ for top NITs.
timeline: 2 yrs M.Tech; then PhD or premium industry placement after.
salary_range: ₹50K–₹70K/month stipend during M.Tech; ₹20L–₹50L placement after.
growth_and_progression: M.Tech → IIT PhD → faculty or research scientist; or → premium industry.
demand_and_outlook: Moderate; IIT M.Tech brand opens senior SDE and research doors.
honest_caveat: GATE preparation takes 6–12 months seriously; stipend is modest.

children.name: MS or PhD Abroad
children.description: Pursue a master's or doctoral degree at a US, European, or Canadian university — the route to AI research, global tech careers, and academic positions.

children.name: MS in Computer Science (USA or Canada)
children.description: One-and-a-half to two-year master's focused on coursework and applied projects — primary route to top US tech company placements and global career options.
children.salaryRange: ₹30L–₹80L / yr returning India salary; $100K–$180K in USA
children.roadmap: B.Tech → GRE and TOEFL → applications → MS admit → US tech internship → full-time offer → return to India or stay abroad

children.name: PhD in CS at Top Global University
children.description: Five-year funded research doctorate — the highest credential in computer science, leading to faculty positions, research labs, or principal IC roles at top tech firms.
skills: deep specialisation (AI or systems or theory)
children.salaryRange: ₹40L–₹1Cr+ / yr post-PhD at research labs or faculty; stipend during PhD
children.roadmap: B.Tech → research publication or project → PhD admit with funding → 5 yrs research → post-doc optional → faculty or research scientist

what_it_is: Graduate study at foreign universities for research depth and global network.
who_its_not_for: Those unwilling to relocate, manage visa complexity, or study for 2–5 more yrs.
work_lifestyle: Research lab; TA or RA responsibilities; intellectually intense and autonomous.
entry_route: Strong CGPA, research publications or projects, GRE, and IELTS or TOEFL.
timeline: MS 1.5–2 yrs; PhD 4–6 yrs; both lead to premium global or India roles.
salary_range: $80K–$200K+ post-MS or PhD in the USA; ₹30L–₹80L+ returning to India.
growth_and_progression: Research scientist at Google or Meta; faculty; or senior IC at tech firms.
demand_and_outlook: High; top global companies prefer PhD and MS from T20 universities.
honest_caveat: Visa uncertainty and cost of living are real; fully-funded PhD is the safer route.

children.name: Government Research and PSU Technology
children.description: Join DRDO, ISRO, C-DAC, or NIC as a scientist or technical officer — stable, mission-driven careers in India's defence and public-sector technology organisations.

children.name: DRDO Scientist (CS or Electronics)
children.description: Work on radar software, cyber defence, communication systems, and avionics at DRDO labs — a prestigious government science career with full job security.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹18L / yr Scientist B to D scale with all allowances
children.roadmap: B.Tech CSE → DRDO CEPTAM exam → Scientist B joining → project lab → Scientist C on performance → senior scientist

children.name: ISRO Scientist or Engineer (Software)
children.description: Build flight software, mission planning systems, and ground control software for India's space programme — one of the most inspiring government CS roles.
children.salaryRange: ₹8L–₹18L / yr scientist or engineer SC scale
children.roadmap: B.Tech CSE → ISRO ICRB exam → technical interview → Scientist or Engineer SC → project at VSSC, ISAC, or ISTRAC → senior scientist

what_it_is: Building India's strategic technology in defence, space, and public infrastructure.
who_its_not_for: Those who want commercial salaries, fast promotions, or publications freedom.
work_lifestyle: Lab-based; classified or sensitive projects; structured government pace.
entry_route: DRDO CEPTAM or SET; ISRO ICRB; C-DAC recruitment after B.Tech.
timeline: B.Tech then exam; Scientist B joining; promotion every 4–5 yrs after.
salary_range: ₹8L–₹18L / yr 7th Pay Commission with research and project allowances.
growth_and_progression: Scientist B → C → D → E → Distinguished Scientist over 20+ years.
demand_and_outlook: Limited seats; ECE and CS always in active recruitment at DRDO and ISRO.
honest_caveat: Classified work limits external publications and industry visibility.

what_it_is: M.Tech, PhD, MS abroad, and research scientist paths for CS graduates.
who_its_not_for: Those who need high income immediately after B.Tech graduation.
work_lifestyle: Research labs; seminar-driven; publication cycles; deeply self-directed.
entry_route: GATE for M.Tech at IITs or NITs; GRE for MS abroad; research internships.
timeline: M.Tech 2 yrs; PhD 4–5 yrs; MS abroad 1.5–2 yrs; all open different doors.
salary_range: ₹5L–₹8L stipend during M.Tech or PhD; ₹20L–₹80L+ post-PhD in industry.
growth_and_progression: Research scientist, IIT faculty, or high-skill industry pivot after PhD.
demand_and_outlook: Niche but growing; India's deep-tech ambitions increasing research appetite.
honest_caveat: Long runway; PhD especially requires tolerance for years of uncertainty.

children.name: Entrepreneurship and Adjacent Tech Careers
children.description: Founding startups, switching into product management, or building independent income as a CS graduate leveraging technical depth.

children.name: Product Management
children.description: Own the vision, roadmap, and outcomes for a software product — translating customer needs into engineering priorities at tech companies.

children.name: PM at Product Company (B2C)
children.description: Own consumer-facing features at companies like Swiggy, Zomato, PhonePe, or Meesho — high velocity, data-driven, and deeply cross-functional product work.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → 2–3 yrs SDE → internal PM switch or APM programme → PM-1 → senior PM in 2–3 yrs → group PM

children.name: PM at B2B or SaaS Company
children.description: Define product roadmap and manage enterprise client requirements at SaaS, fintech infra, or B2B tech companies — more process-driven than B2C but equally high-paying.
children.roadmap: B.Tech → 2–3 yrs backend SDE → PM switch → product manager at B2B SaaS → senior PM → director of product

what_it_is: Defining what gets built, why, and when at software product companies.
who_its_not_for: Those who prefer deep technical implementation over strategy and communication.
work_lifestyle: Cross-functional; no direct reports early; heavy stakeholder alignment work.
entry_route: 2–3 yrs SDE then internal switch; or APM programme at Google or Microsoft.
timeline: APM or PM in 3 yrs post-B.Tech; senior PM in 5–6 yrs; GPM in 8–10.
salary_range: ₹18L–₹50L at senior PM level; ₹50L–₹1Cr+ at director and CPO level.
growth_and_progression: APM → PM → senior PM → group PM → VP product → CPO.
demand_and_outlook: High; experienced technical PMs with shipping track record always scarce.
honest_caveat: Direct B.Tech entry very rare; SDE experience or MBA almost always required.

children.name: Tech Entrepreneurship
children.description: Start or join early-stage tech startups as a founder or first technical employee — the highest-risk, highest-reward path for CS graduates.

children.name: B2C Tech Startup Founder
children.description: Build a consumer-facing app in edtech, fintech, healthtech, or consumer services targeting India's 700M+ internet users — the classic CS founder playbook.
children.salaryRange: ₹0–₹15L founder salary; significant equity if venture-backed and growing
children.roadmap: B.Tech → 2 yrs SDE at startup → identify consumer problem → co-founder + MVP → YC or pre-seed → product-market fit → scale

children.name: B2B or DeepTech Startup Founder
